system-characteristics.description: Vorion Cognigate is an AI Agent Governance Runtime implementing the BASIS (Behavioral AI Safety Interoperability Standard) specification. It provides real-time intent normalization, policy enforcement, trust scoring, and cryptographic proof generation for autonomous AI agent operations. Cognigate serves as an inherited control enforcement layer for AI execution environments, ensuring that every agent action is authorized, bounded, and auditable.

information-types.title: AI Agent Telemetry
information-types.description: Operational telemetry from AI agent interactions including intent declarations, policy evaluation results, trust scores, and enforcement decisions.

information-types.title: Governance Decisions
information-types.description: Records of allow/deny/escalate/modify decisions made by the policy engine, including the full decision rationale and policy chain evaluated.

integrity-impact.adjustment-justification: Governance decisions are cryptographically sealed in the PROOF ledger with SHA-256 hash chain integrity, providing tamper-evidence. Moderate selected due to compensating cryptographic controls.

information-types.title: Trust Scores
information-types.description: Dynamic trust scores (0-1000 scale, 8-tier model) computed for each AI agent entity based on behavioral history, policy compliance, and outcome alignment.

status.remarks: Cognigate is operational in production, processing AI agent governance requests via the INTENT -> ENFORCE -> PROOF pipeline.

authorization-boundary.description: The authorization boundary encompasses the Cognigate Engine core, the PROOF Plane (immutable SHA-256 hash chain audit ledger), the Policy Engine (BASIS rule evaluation), the Trust Engine (8-tier trust scoring), and all supporting API infrastructure. The boundary includes the /v1/intent, /v1/enforce, and /v1/proof API surfaces. External AI agents and their host environments are outside the authorization boundary but are subject to Cognigate governance controls when interacting with the system.

network-architecture.description:
Cognigate operates as an API-based microservice architecture deployed on cloud infrastructure. The architecture consists of:

1. API Gateway Layer - TLS-terminated ingress with rate limiting
2. Cognigate Engine - FastAPI application handling INTENT/ENFORCE/PROOF
3. Policy Engine - In-process BASIS rule evaluation engine
4. Trust Engine - Agent trust score computation service
5. PROOF Plane - Append-only cryptographic audit ledger
6. Data Layer - PostgreSQL with encrypted storage
7. Rate Limiting Layer - In-memory velocity engine for request rate tracking

All inter-service communication uses TLS 1.2+ with mutual authentication where applicable.

data-flow.description:
Primary data flow follows the INTENT -> ENFORCE -> PROOF -> CHAIN pipeline:

1. INTENT: Agent submits action intention via /v1/intent. The intent is normalized, validated, and assigned a unique intent_id.

2. ENFORCE: Normalized intent is evaluated against active BASIS policies. The policy engine checks trust level, permission scope, resource boundaries, and behavioral constraints. Result is allow/deny/escalate/modify.

3. PROOF: Enforcement verdict is cryptographically sealed into a proof record with SHA-256 content hashing and Ed25519 digital signatures.

4. CHAIN: Proof record is appended to the immutable SHA-256 hash chain with forward-linking integrity verification.

inventory-items.description: FastAPI application server serving as the primary HTTP runtime for Cognigate. Handles request routing, middleware execution, OpenAPI schema generation, and lifecycle management for all /v1 API endpoints.

Policy Engine implementing the BASIS (Behavioral AI Safety Interoperability Standard) rule evaluation framework. Evaluates agent intents against active policies including trust-level requirements, permission scopes, resource boundaries, and behavioral constraints.

Trust Engine computing dynamic trust scores for AI agent entities on a 0-1000 scale across an 8-tier model. Evaluates behavioral history, policy compliance, and outcome alignment to determine agent capability boundaries.

Critic AI Engine providing independent secondary evaluation of governance decisions. Invokes an external AI provider to assess policy enforcement outcomes, identify edge cases, and flag potential governance gaps.

PROOF Ledger implementing an append-only SHA-256 hash chain cryptographic audit trail. Generates tamper-evident proof records with SHA-256 content hashing and Ed25519 digital signatures for every governance decision.

Circuit Breaker providing fault isolation and graceful degradation for Cognigate subsystems. Monitors failure rates, implements half-open recovery patterns, and prevents cascading failures across governance pipeline stages.

Evidence Mapper translating runtime governance events into NIST SP 800-53 control evidence. Maps PROOF ledger entries, policy evaluations, and trust score changes to specific control implementations for continuous compliance monitoring.

Control Health Engine computing real-time health scores for NIST SP 800-53 controls based on evidence freshness, coverage completeness, and anomaly detection. Provides continuous authorization readiness assessment.

Intent Router handling /v1/intent API endpoints. Receives raw agent intent declarations, performs initial validation, normalizes intent structure to BASIS format, and routes to the enforcement pipeline.
Enforce Router handling /v1/enforce API endpoints. Orchestrates policy evaluation, trust verification, and enforcement verdict generation for normalized agent intents.
Proof Router handling /v1/proof API endpoints. Provides read access to the PROOF ledger including proof record retrieval, chain integrity verification, and audit trail queries.
Admin Router handling /v1/admin API endpoints. Provides system administration capabilities including health checks, control status queries, configuration management, and operational monitoring.

Vercel Edge Network providing CDN, API Gateway, TLS termination, DDoS protection, and Edge Functions runtime. Serves as the ingress layer for all Cognigate API traffic with global edge distribution and automatic HTTPS.

Neon PostgreSQL managed serverless database providing persistent storage for governance decisions, trust scores, policy configurations, and PROOF ledger records. Features automated backups, connection pooling, and encrypted storage at rest.

External AI Provider (OpenAI/Anthropic) used by the Critic AI Engine for independent secondary evaluation of governance decisions. Accessed via API with TLS encryption in transit. No persistent data stored with the provider.

components.title: Cognigate Engine
components.description: Core FastAPI application implementing the INTENT -> ENFORCE -> PROOF pipeline. Handles intent normalization, policy evaluation dispatch, and proof record creation. Built with Python/FastAPI.

components.title: PROOF Plane
components.description: Immutable SHA-256 hash chain audit ledger. Creates cryptographically sealed records (PROOF = Persistent Record of Operational Facts) with SHA-256 content hashing, Ed25519 digital signatures, and append-only chain integrity. Each record links to its predecessor via previous_hash, forming a tamper-evident chain.

components.title: Policy Engine
components.description: BASIS rule evaluation engine. Evaluates agent intents against active governance policies including trust-level gates, permission scope checks, resource boundary enforcement, behavioral constraint validation, and velocity/rate limiting. Supports policy hot-reload and versioned policy chains.

components.title: Trust Engine
components.description: Computes and maintains dynamic trust scores for AI agent entities. Implements an 8-tier trust model (0-1000 scale): Sandbox (0-199), Observed (200-349), Provisional (350-499), Monitored (500-649), Standard (650-799), Trusted (800-875), Certified (876-950), Autonomous (951-1000). Trust scores influence policy evaluation and enforcement decisions.

control-implementation.description: This section describes how NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5 Moderate baseline controls are implemented within the Vorion Cognigate AI Agent Governance Engine. Implementation statements reference the Cognigate Engine, PROOF Plane, Policy Engine, and Trust Engine components. Evidence is continuously collected via the PROOF ledger and control health monitoring.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AC-1 (Policy and Procedures) as part of the Access Control control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AC-1 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Cognigate implements access control policy through the BASIS Policy Engine (app/core/policy_engine.py), which enforces expression-based constraints across three default policy sets: basis-core-security, basis-data-protection, and basis-risk-thresholds. Policy versioning is maintained through Git-tracked ADR documentation (17 ADRs) and the PolicyEngine.load_policies_from_file() method supports YAML/JSON policy definitions. Access control procedures are enforced at the API boundary through API key authentication on all /v1/* endpoints and X-Admin-Key requirements on administrative endpoints (/v1/admin/*). Policy review and update is triggered by configuration mutations tracked in the PROOF ledger. The policy engine supports programmatic registration (register_policy/unregister_policy), file-based loading, and default policy initialization during application lifespan startup in app/main.py.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate provides the policy enforcement mechanism and three default BASIS policies. The consumer organization is responsible for defining organization-specific access control policies beyond the default BASIS policy set, establishing review cadence, and maintaining policy documentation.

by-components.description: Account management for Cognigate is handled through centralized IAM with automated provisioning. Agent entities are registered via the /v1/intent API with unique entity_ids. Access reviews are conducted quarterly. Agent accounts are automatically suspended when trust scores drop below the Sandbox tier (0-199).
Continuous evidence for AC-2 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Account management is implemented through the Agent Management system (app/routers/agents.py) via /v1/agents endpoints. Entity registration creates identity records with Ed25519 key pairs (app/core/signatures.py) and assigns initial trust scores based on observation tier: BLACK_BOX (score 100, T0 Sandbox), GRAY_BOX (score 200, T1 Observed), WHITE_BOX (score 350, T2 Provisional). Agent lifecycle operations include registration (POST /v1/agents), listing (GET /v1/agents), detail retrieval (GET /v1/agents/{id}), update (PATCH /v1/agents/{id}), and revocation (DELETE /v1/agents/{id}). Revoked agents are soft-deleted (isRevoked=true) and blocked from further operations. API key management (app/routers/auth_keys.py) provides key creation with SHA-256 hashing (raw keys never stored), listing, and revocation via /v1/auth/keys endpoints. Entity halt functionality via the circuit breaker (app/core/circuit_breaker.py) enables immediate account suspension through /v1/admin/entity/halt and cascade halt for parent-child agent trees via /v1/admin/entity/cascade-halt.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate manages AI agent accounts and API keys with full lifecycle support. The consumer organization is responsible for defining account management policies, provisioning human administrator accounts, and establishing account review schedules.

by-components.description: Automated account management is implemented through the trust decay system with a 182-day half-life (trust_decay_rate: 0.01 in app/config.py). Agent accounts that do not produce positive trust signals automatically decay toward T0 Sandbox tier. The velocity tracker (app/core/velocity.py) automatically throttles entities exceeding rate limits, and the circuit breaker automatically halts entities exceeding violation thresholds (entity_violation_threshold: 10 in CircuitConfig). Observation ceilings enforce maximum tier caps: BLACK_BOX agents cannot exceed T2, GRAY_BOX cannot exceed T4, limiting privilege accumulation. Trust signal history is maintained per agent via /v1/trust/{agent_id}/history for audit review.
by-components.remarks: Fully automated through trust decay, velocity tracking, and circuit breaker entity management.

by-components.description: Temporary account management is implemented through agent expiration in the registration system (app/routers/agents.py). Each registered agent receives an expiresAt timestamp (default: 24 hours from admission). Emergency accounts are managed through the circuit breaker's manual halt/reset capabilities (app/routers/admin.py) and trust revocation endpoint (POST /v1/trust/{agent_id}/revoke), which immediately sets trust score to 0 and marks the agent as revoked.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate provides expiration and emergency revocation mechanisms. Consumer organization must establish procedures for temporary account creation and emergency account protocols.

by-components.description: Account disabling is implemented through multiple mechanisms: (1) Agent revocation via DELETE /v1/agents/{agent_id} sets isRevoked=true, preventing further operations; (2) Entity halt via POST /v1/admin/entity/halt adds the entity to the halted_entities set in the circuit breaker, blocking all requests; (3) Trust revocation via POST /v1/trust/{agent_id}/revoke sets score to 0 and tier to T0_SANDBOX; (4) API key deletion via DELETE /v1/auth/keys/{key_id} permanently removes the authentication credential. Disabled accounts are logged via structlog with entity_halted or admin_entity_halt events.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate provides multiple disable mechanisms. Consumer organization defines conditions under which accounts are disabled and retention periods.

by-components.description: All account management actions are automatically recorded in the PROOF ledger (app/db/proof_repository.py) as immutable, hash-chained records with Ed25519 signatures. The evidence mapper (app/core/evidence_mapper.py) automatically maps TRUST_DELTA events to NIST 800-53 AC-2 compliance evidence. Trust signal recording (POST /v1/trust/{agent_id}/signal) logs every trust adjustment with type, source, weight, delta, context, and timestamp. The circuit breaker logs entity_halted and entity_unhalted events with structlog JSON formatting for SIEM integration.
by-components.remarks: Fully automated audit logging through the PROOF chain and evidence mapper.

by-components.description: Access enforcement is implemented through the Cognigate Policy Engine. Every agent action passes through the ENFORCE pipeline, which evaluates trust level gates, permission scope checks, resource boundary enforcement, and behavioral constraints. Deny-by-default is the foundational policy.
Continuous evidence for AC-3 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Access enforcement is implemented through the ENFORCE layer (app/routers/enforce.py), which is the central policy decision point for all agent actions. Every action request passes through a multi-layer enforcement pipeline: (1) Circuit Breaker check - system-wide halt verification; (2) Velocity Cap check - L0 burst, L1 sustained, L2 hourly/daily rate limits per trust tier; (3) Policy Evaluation via PolicyEngine.evaluate() - expression-based constraint checking against loaded policies. The ENFORCE endpoint (POST /v1/enforce) returns one of four verdicts: allow, deny, escalate, or modify. Critical violations result in immediate denial, high violations trigger escalation requiring human approval, and medium/low violations permit with audit logging. Administrative endpoints are protected by X-Admin-Key authentication with constant-time comparison (secrets.compare_digest) in app/core/auth.py, returning HTTP 401 for missing keys and HTTP 403 for invalid keys.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate provides comprehensive access enforcement as its core function. Every agent action is subject to policy evaluation before execution.

by-components.description: Information flow enforcement is implemented through network segmentation, API gateway controls, and the Cognigate ENFORCE pipeline. Data flows follow the INTENT -> ENFORCE -> PROOF -> CHAIN path with each stage applying flow control policies.
Continuous evidence for AC-4 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Information flow enforcement is implemented through the capability taxonomy and data classification system. The INTENT layer (app/routers/intent.py) classifies data types involved in each action (pii_email, credentials, pii_ssn) and the ENFORCE layer evaluates data flow constraints through the basis-data-protection policy. The policy constraint 'pii-requires-l2' blocks PII access for entities below T2 Provisional trust level. The constraint 'credentials-audit' requires audit logging for any credential access. CORS middleware in app/main.py controls cross-origin information flows. GZip compression middleware (minimum_size=500) processes all responses uniformly. The gateway proxy (app/routers/gateway.py) enforces information flow boundaries between Cognigate and external AgentAnchor services.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate enforces data classification-based flow controls through policy constraints. Consumer organization defines additional data flow policies specific to their information types.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AC-5 (Separation of Duties) as part of the Access Control control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AC-5 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Separation of duties is enforced through the architectural separation of the INTENT, ENFORCE, and PROOF layers. The INTENT layer (app/routers/intent.py) analyzes and normalizes goals but cannot execute them. The ENFORCE layer (app/routers/enforce.py) evaluates policies but does not perform the analyzed actions. The PROOF layer (app/routers/proof.py) records decisions immutably but cannot modify enforcement outcomes. The AI Critic (app/core/critic.py) provides independent adversarial evaluation separate from the planning function (Planner + Critic pattern). Administrative operations require separate X-Admin-Key authentication distinct from agent API keys. Trust signal recording and trust scoring are separated from enforcement decisions.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate enforces architectural separation of duties across its pipeline layers. Consumer organization establishes role-based separation for human administrators.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AC-6 (Least Privilege) as part of the Access Control control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AC-6 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Least privilege is enforced through the 8-tier trust model (T0 Sandbox through T7 Autonomous) defined in app/constants_bridge.py. Each trust tier unlocks progressively more capabilities, with lower tiers having strict restrictions. The basis-core-security policy enforces 'no-shell-low-trust' (shell execution requires T3+ Monitored trust level) and 'no-delete-without-approval' (file deletion always requires human approval). Rigor modes (STRICT for T0-T2, STANDARD for T3-T4, LITE for T5-T7) in app/routers/enforce.py apply proportional scrutiny based on trust level. Velocity limits in app/core/velocity.py scale with trust tier (T0: 2 actions/second, T7: 500 actions/second). Observation ceilings (BLACK_BOX: max T2, GRAY_BOX: max T4, WHITE_BOX: max T7) prevent excessive privilege accumulation.
by-components.remarks: Least privilege is a core architectural principle of Cognigate, enforced through trust tiers, capability gates, velocity caps, and rigor modes.

by-components.description: Access to security functions is restricted through X-Admin-Key authentication (app/core/auth.py) required on all /v1/admin/* endpoints. Security-critical operations including circuit breaker halt/reset (POST /v1/admin/circuit/halt, /reset), entity halt/unhalt (POST /v1/admin/entity/halt, /unhalt), cascade halt (POST /v1/admin/entity/cascade-halt), velocity throttle/unthrottle (POST /v1/admin/velocity/throttle, /unthrottle), and compliance monitoring triggers (POST /v1/compliance/monitor/trigger) all require admin authentication. The admin API key is configured via the ADMIN_API_KEY environment variable with a production default warning ('CHANGE_ME_IN_PRODUCTION').
by-components.remarks: Cognigate restricts security function access to authenticated administrators. Consumer organization must configure strong admin API keys and limit key distribution.

by-components.description: Non-security functions are accessible without admin privileges. Public endpoints including /health, /health/live, /ready, /status, /docs, /redoc, and all /v1/compliance/health/* endpoints operate without authentication. Agent operations (/v1/agents, /v1/trust, /v1/intent, /v1/enforce, /v1/proof) use standard API key authentication distinct from admin keys. The optional_admin_key dependency in app/core/auth.py allows endpoints to offer enhanced functionality for admins while providing base functionality for non-privileged users.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate separates privileged and non-privileged access across all endpoints.

by-components.description: Privileged access is restricted to the X-Admin-Key authentication mechanism (app/core/auth.py). Only holders of the admin API key can execute privileged operations including system halt, entity management, and velocity control. The verify_admin_key dependency uses constant-time comparison (secrets.compare_digest) to prevent timing attacks against the admin key. Failed admin authentication attempts are logged as admin_auth_missing_key or admin_auth_invalid_key events. The admin key is never logged or exposed in API responses.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate provides a single privileged access mechanism with timing-attack resistance. Consumer organization defines policies for admin key distribution and rotation.

by-components.description: All privileged function usage is logged through structlog JSON-formatted logging. Admin circuit breaker operations generate admin_circuit_halt and admin_circuit_reset log events. Entity management generates admin_entity_halt, admin_entity_unhalt, and admin_cascade_halt events. Velocity control generates admin_throttle and admin_unthrottle events. Each log event includes the operation performed, target entity, reason, and timestamp in structured JSON format suitable for SIEM aggregation.
by-components.remarks: All privileged function invocations are automatically logged with structured JSON output.

by-components.description: Non-privileged users are prohibited from executing privileged functions through the FastAPI Depends(verify_admin_key) dependency injection pattern. All admin router endpoints in app/routers/admin.py require this dependency, which returns HTTP 401 (AuthError) if no key is provided and HTTP 403 (ForbiddenError) if an invalid key is provided. The compliance monitor trigger endpoint also requires admin authentication. Non-admin users cannot access circuit breaker controls, entity halt/unhalt, or velocity throttle operations.
by-components.remarks: Enforced at the framework level through FastAPI dependency injection - cannot be bypassed by non-privileged callers.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AC-7 (Unsuccessful Logon Attempts) as part of the Access Control control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AC-7 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Unsuccessful access attempts are managed through the velocity tracking system (app/core/velocity.py) and circuit breaker (app/core/circuit_breaker.py). The velocity tracker enforces per-entity rate limits across four tiers: L0 burst (per-second), L1 sustained (per-minute), L2 hourly, and L2 daily. Entities exceeding rate limits are automatically blocked with a retry-after duration. The circuit breaker tracks entity_violations and automatically halts entities exceeding the violation threshold (entity_violation_threshold: 10). Invalid admin API key attempts return HTTP 403 and are logged as admin_auth_invalid_key events. The injection_threshold (2 attempts) in the circuit breaker triggers a system-wide halt when injection attacks are detected.
by-components.remarks: Automated lockout through velocity caps and circuit breaker entity halt. Configurable thresholds via CircuitConfig.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AC-8 (System Use Notification) as part of the Access Control control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AC-8 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

System use notification is provided through the API documentation system. The FastAPI application in app/main.py serves OpenAPI documentation at /docs and /redoc with a comprehensive description of the BASIS governance pipeline, security layers, and usage terms. The landing page (GET /) displays system identification including 'Cognigate Engine' with version, Apache 2.0 license terms, and links to the BASIS standard. The /status endpoint returns machine-readable service identification. The API description explicitly states the enforcement pipeline: 'Every agent action passes through INTENT, ENFORCE, and PROOF before it touches the world.'
by-components.remarks: Cognigate provides system identification and usage documentation. Consumer organization is responsible for implementing specific system use notification banners and consent mechanisms for their deployment.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AC-11 (Device Lock) as part of the Access Control control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AC-11 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Device lock is implemented at the session level through API key expiration and agent expiration mechanisms. Registered agents receive an expiresAt timestamp (default: 24 hours). API keys do not include built-in session timeout, but the access_token_expire_minutes setting (30 minutes) in app/config.py provides session expiration capability. The circuit breaker's auto_reset_seconds (300 seconds) provides automatic session re-establishment after system halts.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate is an API service rather than an interactive terminal. Session locking is implemented through token expiration. Consumer organization implements device lock at their user-facing application layer.

by-components.description: Pattern-hiding is implemented in the API key management system (app/routers/auth_keys.py). API keys are stored as SHA-256 hashes (_hash_key function) and are never displayed after initial creation. The key listing endpoint (GET /v1/auth/keys) returns only metadata (keyId, name, scopes, createdAt, lastUsedAt) without the key value. The creation response includes a warning: 'Store this key securely. It will not be shown again.' Admin API keys are never included in any API response or log output.
by-components.remarks: Sensitive authentication credentials are never re-displayed after initial creation.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AC-12 (Session Termination) as part of the Access Control control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AC-12 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Session termination is implemented through multiple mechanisms: (1) Agent expiration via expiresAt timestamp in registration; (2) API key revocation via DELETE /v1/auth/keys/{key_id}; (3) Agent revocation via DELETE /v1/agents/{agent_id}; (4) Trust revocation via POST /v1/trust/{agent_id}/revoke setting score to 0; (5) Entity halt via POST /v1/admin/entity/halt blocking all requests; (6) System-wide halt via POST /v1/admin/circuit/halt. The access_token_expire_minutes configuration (default: 30) controls token lifetime. The circuit breaker auto-reset (auto_reset_seconds: 300) provides automatic session re-establishment after safety halts.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate provides multiple session termination mechanisms. Consumer organization establishes session termination policies and timeout values.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AC-14 (Permitted Actions Without Identification) as part of the Access Control control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AC-14 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Cognigate permits specific actions without authentication: (1) Health checks: GET /health, GET /health/live, GET /ready; (2) Status page: GET /status; (3) Documentation: GET /docs, GET /redoc, GET /openapi.json; (4) Compliance health monitoring: GET /v1/compliance/health, /v1/compliance/dashboard, /v1/compliance/snapshot; (5) Reference data: GET /v1/reference/tiers, /v1/reference/capabilities, /v1/reference/errors. All enforcement operations (INTENT, ENFORCE, PROOF) and administrative operations require authentication. Unauthenticated endpoints are read-only and expose no sensitive data.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate explicitly identifies which actions are permitted without authentication. Consumer organization documents these in their security authorization package.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AC-17 (Remote Access) as part of the Access Control control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AC-17 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

All Cognigate access is remote via HTTPS API. CORS middleware in app/main.py controls cross-origin access (currently allow_origins=['*'], configured per deployment). All API endpoints are served over HTTPS when deployed on Vercel or behind a TLS-terminating reverse proxy. Rate limiting via the velocity tracker (app/core/velocity.py) enforces per-entity access limits across three tiers (L0 burst, L1 sustained, L2 quota). Admin remote access requires X-Admin-Key header authentication with constant-time comparison. The gateway_timeout_ms setting (30000ms) prevents hanging remote connections.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate enforces rate-limited, authenticated remote access. Consumer organization configures CORS origins, TLS certificates, and network-level access controls for their deployment.

by-components.description: Remote access monitoring is implemented through the velocity tracker (app/core/velocity.py) which tracks all entity actions with timestamps, counts, and rate calculations. The circuit breaker (app/core/circuit_breaker.py) monitors system-wide request patterns including high_risk_ratio, block_ratio, tripwire_triggers, and injection_attempts. The admin status endpoint (GET /v1/admin/status) provides real-time system health including tracked entities, security layer status, and circuit breaker metrics. The compliance health endpoint (GET /v1/compliance/health) monitors control health across 9 frameworks in real-time. Structlog JSON logging captures all API access for external SIEM analysis.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate provides comprehensive remote access monitoring. Consumer organization integrates logs with their SIEM and establishes monitoring procedures.

by-components.description: Confidentiality and integrity of remote access is protected through HTTPS/TLS encryption enforced at the deployment layer (Vercel edge network or reverse proxy). All proof records are signed with Ed25519 digital signatures (app/core/signatures.py) providing end-to-end integrity. The SHA-256 hash chain in the PROOF layer ensures record integrity is verifiable independently of transport security. API key authentication uses SHA-256 hashing for storage and secrets.compare_digest for comparison, preventing timing-based key extraction. GZip compression middleware (minimum_size=500) is applied after encryption.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate provides application-level integrity through cryptographic signatures. TLS encryption is provided by the hosting infrastructure (Vercel, cloud provider, or reverse proxy).

by-components.description: Privileged remote commands are restricted to X-Admin-Key authenticated endpoints. All administrative operations (circuit breaker halt/reset, entity halt/unhalt, cascade halt, velocity throttle/unthrottle) require the admin API key and are logged with operation-specific structured log events. The compliance monitoring trigger (POST /v1/compliance/monitor/trigger) also requires admin authentication. Each privileged command execution is recorded with the admin operation type, target entity, reason, and timestamp.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate restricts and documents privileged remote access. Consumer organization authorizes remote privileged access and maintains access records.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AC-18 (Wireless Access) as part of the Access Control control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AC-18 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Cognigate is a cloud SaaS application accessed exclusively via HTTPS API. Wireless access controls are inherited from the cloud infrastructure provider (Vercel/AWS) and the consumer organization's network architecture. Cognigate does not directly manage wireless access points or wireless network infrastructure.
by-components.remarks: Wireless infrastructure controls are the responsibility of the cloud provider and consumer organization's network team. Cognigate operates at the application layer.

by-components.description: Wireless authentication and encryption are inherited from the cloud infrastructure provider. Cognigate enforces application-layer authentication via API keys and admin keys regardless of the underlying network transport.
by-components.remarks: Inherited from cloud infrastructure provider.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AC-19 (Access Control for Mobile Devices) as part of the Access Control control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AC-19 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Cognigate is a cloud SaaS API service. Mobile device access controls are inherited from the consumer organization's mobile device management policies. Cognigate enforces the same API authentication and rate limiting regardless of the client device type.
by-components.remarks: Mobile device management is the responsibility of the consumer organization. Cognigate applies uniform access controls regardless of device type.

by-components.description: Mobile device encryption is inherited from the consumer organization's mobile device management policies. Cognigate does not store data on mobile devices.
by-components.remarks: Inherited from consumer organization's mobile device management.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AC-20 (Use of External Systems) as part of the Access Control control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AC-20 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

External system usage is controlled through the gateway proxy (app/routers/gateway.py) which mediates all connections to the AgentAnchor platform. The gateway includes timeout controls (gateway_timeout_ms: 30000), circuit breaker protection (gateway_circuit_breaker_threshold: 5), and proxies only to whitelisted domains (compliance, council, observer, truth-chain, academy, orchestrator, webhooks, dashboard). The AI Critic (app/core/critic.py) connects to external AI providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, xAI) with configurable API keys and model selection. All external connections are logged for audit.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate controls and monitors external system connections through the gateway and critic subsystems. Consumer organization establishes policies for approved external systems.

by-components.description: External system usage is limited through configuration controls. The gateway proxy only accepts connections to predefined AgentAnchor API domains. The critic provider is configured through the critic_provider setting with explicit API key requirements per provider. The gateway circuit breaker threshold (5 failures) automatically disables external connections when reliability degrades. External API timeouts (gateway_timeout_ms: 30000) prevent resource exhaustion from slow external systems.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate limits external system connections to configured endpoints. Consumer organization defines acceptable use policies for external systems.

by-components.description: Cognigate is a cloud SaaS application and does not interact with portable storage devices. This control is not applicable to the Cognigate runtime.
by-components.remarks: Cloud SaaS application — portable storage device controls are the responsibility of the consumer organization and cloud infrastructure provider.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AC-21 (Information Sharing) as part of the Access Control control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AC-21 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Information sharing controls are implemented through data classification in the INTENT layer and enforced by the ENFORCE layer. The intent analysis (app/routers/intent.py) automatically classifies data types (pii_email, credentials, pii_ssn) and the basis-data-protection policy enforces sharing constraints: PII requires T2+ trust, credential access requires audit logging. The PROOF chain records all information sharing decisions with cryptographic integrity, providing non-repudiation. The evidence mapper (app/core/evidence_mapper.py) generates compliance records for each sharing decision across 7 frameworks.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate classifies and controls information sharing through policy enforcement. Consumer organization defines specific sharing agreements and authorized recipients.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AC-22 (Publicly Accessible Content) as part of the Access Control control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AC-22 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Publicly accessible content is controlled through endpoint authentication requirements. Unauthenticated endpoints serve only system operational data: health status, compliance posture, reference data (trust tiers, capabilities, error codes), API documentation, and the landing page. No agent data, proof records, enforcement decisions, or trust scores are accessible without authentication. The robots.txt (app/main.py) controls search engine indexing. The sitemap.xml lists only public documentation pages.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate restricts publicly accessible content to operational and documentation endpoints. Consumer organization reviews and authorizes what content is publicly accessible in their deployment.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AT-1 (Policy and Procedures) as part of the Awareness and Training control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AT-1 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Cognigate supports awareness and training policy through comprehensive API documentation (/docs, /redoc), interactive developer tools (/tools/calculator, /tools/errors, /tools/sdks, /tools/playground), and the landing page which explains the BASIS governance pipeline (BASIS -> CAR -> INTENT -> ENFORCE -> PROOF). The trust calculator allows administrators to explore the T0-T7 trust model interactively. The error codes reference provides searchable documentation for 38+ enforcement error codes. SDK quickstart guides are available for TypeScript, Python, Go, and cURL.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate provides extensive technical documentation and training tools. The consumer organization is responsible for establishing security awareness and training programs, defining training schedules, and maintaining training records.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AT-2 (Literacy Training and Awareness) as part of the Awareness and Training control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AT-2 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Security awareness content is embedded in Cognigate's documentation and developer tools. The API documentation (/docs) includes security layer descriptions (tripwires, paranoia mode, critic, circuit breaker, velocity caps). The error codes reference (/tools/errors) educates developers on security enforcement behavior. The trust calculator (/tools/calculator) helps administrators understand trust tier implications. The playground (/tools/playground) allows testing the full pipeline to understand security enforcement behavior before production deployment.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate provides security literacy content through its developer tools. Consumer organization establishes formal awareness training programs for their users.

by-components.description: Insider threat awareness is inherent in Cognigate's adversarial security design. The AI Critic (app/core/critic.py) operates on an 'ASSUME BAD INTENT until proven otherwise' principle, looking for euphemisms, scope creep, and dangerous tool combinations. The tripwire system (app/core/tripwires.py) detects credential theft patterns (cat_shadow, export_ssh_keys), privilege escalation (chmod_world_writable, chown_root), and history destruction (history_clear). The circuit breaker entity misbehavior detection tracks per-entity violations and automatically halts entities showing suspicious patterns.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate's adversarial design addresses insider threat detection. Consumer organization establishes insider threat awareness training programs.

by-components.description: Social engineering detection is implemented through the INTENT layer's paranoia mode (app/routers/intent.py), which detects euphemisms that may indicate social engineering attempts. Euphemism keywords ('clear', 'clean', 'organize', 'tidy', 'archive', 'free up', 'sanitize') combined with system paths trigger critical risk scores (0.95). The AI Critic evaluates requests for 'euphemisms or obfuscation' and 'what is the WORST interpretation of this request.' The tripwire system detects curl-pipe-bash patterns (curl_bash) which are common social engineering attack vectors.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate detects social engineering patterns in agent requests. Consumer organization provides social engineering awareness training to human users.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AT-3 (Role-Based Training) as part of the Awareness and Training control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AT-3 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Role-based training content is provided through tier-specific documentation. The trust tier reference (/v1/reference/tiers) documents the 8-tier trust model with per-tier capabilities, velocity limits, and rigor modes. The capabilities reference (/v1/reference/capabilities) documents 100+ capabilities across 8 namespaces. The rate limits reference (/v1/reference/rate-limits) documents per-tier API limits. SDK quickstart guides (/tools/sdks) provide role-appropriate integration guidance for developers in TypeScript, Python, Go, and cURL.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate provides role-specific technical documentation. Consumer organization establishes formal role-based security training programs with completion tracking.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AT-4 (Training Records) as part of the Awareness and Training control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AT-4 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Training record management is not directly implemented within Cognigate. Cognigate provides the trust signal history (GET /v1/trust/{agent_id}/history) which records behavioral learning for AI agents, analogous to training records for automated entities. The proof chain maintains a complete record of all agent interactions that can serve as evidence of system usage and behavioral patterns.
by-components.remarks: Training record management for human users is the responsibility of the consumer organization's HR and training management systems. Cognigate records agent behavioral history as an analogue for automated entity training.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AU-1 (Policy and Procedures) as part of the Audit and Accountability control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AU-1 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Audit and accountability policy is implemented through the PROOF layer (app/routers/proof.py, app/db/proof_repository.py), which maintains an immutable, cryptographically-signed audit chain. The audit policy is codified in the evidence mapper (app/core/evidence_mapper.py) which defines retention periods per framework: NIST 800-53 (7 years), EU AI Act (10 years), SOC 2 (5 years), GDPR (6 years). Audit procedures are enforced automatically — every INTENT, ENFORCE, and PROOF operation generates structured log records via structlog with ISO timestamps, log levels, and JSON formatting. The proof chain records 6+ event types: INTENT_RECEIVED, DECISION_MADE, TRUST_DELTA, EXECUTION_STARTED, EXECUTION_COMPLETED, EXECUTION_FAILED, TRIPWIRE_TRIGGERED, CIRCUIT_BREAKER_OPEN, CIRCUIT_BREAKER_CLOSE, VELOCITY_EXCEEDED, CRITIC_VERDICT.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate provides the audit mechanism and retention enforcement. Consumer organization establishes audit review procedures and assigns audit responsibilities.

by-components.description: Cognigate logs all security-relevant events including: authentication attempts, authorization decisions (allow/deny/escalate/modify), policy evaluations, trust score changes, proof record creation, chain integrity verification, and administrative actions. Events are structured as JSON via structlog.
Continuous evidence for AU-2 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Event logging is comprehensive across all Cognigate subsystems. The PROOF chain captures: intent submissions (INTENT_RECEIVED), enforcement decisions (DECISION_MADE), trust changes (TRUST_DELTA), execution lifecycle (EXECUTION_STARTED/COMPLETED/FAILED), security events (TRIPWIRE_TRIGGERED), system halts (CIRCUIT_BREAKER_OPEN/CLOSE), rate limit violations (VELOCITY_EXCEEDED), and AI evaluations (CRITIC_VERDICT). Each event is recorded with entity_id, action_type, decision (allowed/denied/escalated/modified), inputs_hash, outputs_hash, chain position, and Ed25519 signature. Structlog (configured in app/main.py) provides additional operational logging with ISO timestamps, log levels, and structured JSON output. The control health engine (app/core/control_health.py) monitors AU-2 compliance in real-time.
by-components.remarks: Comprehensive event logging is a core architectural component. All governance pipeline events are automatically logged without requiring consumer configuration.

by-components.description: All audit records contain: timestamp (UTC ISO-8601), event type, source component, outcome (decision), entity identity (entity_id), intent identifier, verdict identifier, and additional context. PROOF records additionally include inputs_hash, outputs_hash, previous_hash, and Ed25519 digital signature.
Continuous evidence for AU-3 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Audit records in the PROOF chain (app/models/proof.py) contain: proof_id (unique identifier), chain_position (sequential order), intent_id (associated intent), verdict_id (enforcement decision reference), entity_id (acting entity), action_type (event classification), decision (allowed/denied/escalated/modified), inputs_hash (SHA-256 of inputs), outputs_hash (SHA-256 of outputs), previous_hash (chain linkage), hash (record integrity), signature (Ed25519 digital signature), created_at (UTC timestamp), and metadata (extensible context). Structlog entries include log level, logger name, ISO timestamp, and structured key-value pairs. The evidence mapper enriches events with framework-specific context for NIST, EU AI Act, ISO 42001, SOC 2, NIST AI RMF, CMMC, and GDPR.
by-components.remarks: Audit record content exceeds NIST requirements by including cryptographic integrity fields, SHA-256 hash chain verification, and multi-framework compliance mapping.

by-components.description: Additional audit information is captured through the metadata field in ProofRecord (app/models/proof.py), which accepts extensible key-value pairs. The evidence mapper (app/core/evidence_mapper.py) adds framework-specific metadata including intent_id, verdict_id, entity_id, decision, and chain_position to every ControlEvidence record. The critic verdict (app/core/critic.py) adds AI-generated risk analysis including judgment, confidence, risk_adjustment, hidden_risks, reasoning, and concerns. Trust signal history records type, source, weight, delta, context, and timestamp for each signal.
by-components.remarks: Extensible metadata architecture supports arbitrary additional audit information.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AU-4 (Audit Log Storage Capacity) as part of the Audit and Accountability control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AU-4 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Audit log storage is managed through the database layer (app/db/database.py) supporting PostgreSQL (production via DATABASE_URL/Neon) . The ProofRecordDB model (app/db/models.py) uses indexed columns for efficient querying (proof_id, chain_position, intent_id, verdict_id, entity_id, decision, hash, created_at). Pagination is supported through offset/limit parameters in ProofQuery (limit: 1-1000 records per query). The evidence mapper defines retention periods per framework: 7 years for NIST 800-53, 10 years for EU AI Act. Database scaling is handled by the PostgreSQL provider (Neon serverless PostgreSQL on Vercel).
by-components.remarks: Cognigate provides indexed, paginated audit storage. Storage capacity management is shared with the database infrastructure provider.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AU-5 (Response to Audit Processing Failures) as part of the Audit and Accountability control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AU-5 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Audit logging process failure response is implemented through fault-tolerant initialization in app/main.py. Each subsystem (async logger, database, cache, signatures, policy engine) initializes independently within try/except blocks. If the database initialization fails, it is logged as database_init_failed but does not prevent the application from starting. The async log queue (app/core/async_logger.py) provides buffered logging that survives transient failures. The circuit breaker trips on system anomalies, which includes logging subsystem degradation as part of overall system health assessment. The health endpoint (GET /health) reports subsystem status including database connectivity with latency measurements.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate provides fault-tolerant audit logging with degradation detection. Consumer organization establishes alerting and escalation procedures for audit logging failures.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AU-6 (Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting) as part of the Audit and Accountability control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AU-6 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Audit record review is supported through multiple query interfaces: (1) Proof chain query API (GET /v1/proof) with filters by entity_id, intent_id, verdict_id, decision, date range, and pagination; (2) Proof chain statistics (GET /v1/proof/stats) providing total records, chain length, records by decision type, and chain integrity status; (3) Compliance dashboard (GET /v1/compliance/dashboard) aggregating compliance metrics across 9 frameworks; (4) Trust signal history (GET /v1/trust/{agent_id}/history) for per-agent audit review; (5) Circuit breaker trip history (GET /v1/admin/circuit/history) for security event analysis; (6) Velocity statistics (GET /v1/admin/velocity/{entity_id}) for rate limit analysis. All endpoints return JSON for integration with external SIEM and reporting tools.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate provides comprehensive audit query APIs. Consumer organization establishes review frequency, assigns reviewers, and defines reporting requirements.

by-components.description: Automated audit analysis is implemented through the evidence mapper (app/core/evidence_mapper.py) which automatically maps proof chain events to compliance controls across 7 frameworks without manual intervention. The control health engine (app/core/control_health.py) performs real-time compliance health computation based on subsystem state. The compliance dashboard endpoint (GET /v1/compliance/dashboard) provides automated aggregation of compliance metrics. The circuit breaker (app/core/circuit_breaker.py) performs automated analysis of request patterns (high_risk_ratio, block_ratio, tripwire_triggers) and triggers system-level responses when thresholds are exceeded.
by-components.remarks: Automated compliance mapping and health monitoring operate continuously without manual intervention.

by-components.description: Audit record correlation is implemented through the unified proof chain which links all events via chain_position and previous_hash fields. Each proof record references its associated intent_id and verdict_id, enabling correlation between intent analysis, enforcement decisions, and execution outcomes. The evidence mapper correlates proof events to compliance controls across 7 frameworks simultaneously. The entity_id field enables cross-referencing all actions by a single agent across the entire proof chain. Trust signal history provides temporal correlation of behavioral patterns.
by-components.remarks: The hash-chained proof ledger provides inherent correlation through cryptographic linkage.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AU-7 (Audit Record Reduction and Report Generation) as part of the Audit and Accountability control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AU-7 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Audit record reduction is supported through the ProofQuery model (app/models/proof.py) which provides filtering by entity_id, intent_id, verdict_id, decision type, date range, with configurable limit (1-1000) and offset. The proof statistics endpoint (GET /v1/proof/stats) provides summary reduction including total records, chain length, records_by_decision breakdown, and chain integrity status. The compliance dashboard (GET /v1/compliance/dashboard) generates aggregate reports with per-framework compliance percentages, control counts by status (compliant/degraded/non_compliant/unknown), and recent alerts.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate provides query-based audit reduction and summary reporting. Consumer organization may implement additional reporting formats for their compliance needs.

by-components.description: Automatic audit processing is implemented through the evidence mapper (app/core/evidence_mapper.py) which processes every proof chain event and automatically generates compliance evidence records. The control health engine (app/core/control_health.py) automatically computes health status for 60+ controls across 9 frameworks. The compliance snapshot endpoint (GET /v1/compliance/snapshot) generates point-in-time reports with unique snapshot IDs. The framework evidence export endpoint (GET /v1/compliance/evidence/export/{framework}) exports all evidence for auditor consumption.
by-components.remarks: Fully automated evidence generation, health computation, and report generation.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AU-8 (Time Stamps) as part of the Audit and Accountability control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AU-8 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Timestamps are generated using Python's datetime.utcnow() and datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc) for all audit records. The ProofRecord model (app/models/proof.py) records created_at with UTC timestamps. Structlog is configured with TimeStamper(fmt='iso') in app/main.py for ISO 8601 formatted timestamps on all log entries. The health endpoint reports uptime_seconds using time.monotonic() for drift-resistant uptime tracking. Trust signal records, agent registrations, and all database records include datetime timestamps with index support for temporal queries.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate generates UTC timestamps for all audit records. Time synchronization with authoritative sources (NTP) is managed by the hosting infrastructure.

by-components.description: The PROOF Plane implements immutable audit records via a SHA-256 hash chain. Records are append-only with cryptographic integrity verification. Each record links to its predecessor via previous_hash. Ed25519 signatures provide non-repudiation. Access to PROOF records is read-only for auditors.
Continuous evidence for AU-9 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Audit information is protected through the immutable SHA-256 hash chain . Each proof record includes a previous_hash field linking to the prior record, creating a tamper-evident chain where any modification invalidates all subsequent records. Ed25519 digital signatures (app/core/signatures.py) provide non-repudiation — the signature_manager signs each proof record with a private key that can be loaded from file (PEM), environment variable (base64-encoded), or generated ephemerally. The chain integrity verification endpoint (verify_chain_integrity in app/db/proof_repository.py) validates the entire chain from genesis hash (64 zeros) through every subsequent record. Admin authentication (X-Admin-Key) restricts access to audit management operations.
by-components.remarks: Cryptographic integrity protection is a core architectural feature of the PROOF layer.

by-components.description: Access to audit management functions is restricted to X-Admin-Key holders. The compliance monitoring trigger (POST /v1/compliance/monitor/trigger) requires admin authentication. Proof chain verification is available through the stats endpoint but chain modification is architecturally impossible due to the immutable hash chain design. The circuit breaker trip history (GET /v1/admin/circuit/history) is only accessible with admin authentication. Read access to proof records (GET /v1/proof) is available to authenticated API users but write access is controlled by the internal proof chain append-only architecture.
by-components.remarks: Audit information is append-only by design. Administrative access to audit management is restricted to admin key holders.

by-components.description: Non-repudiation is provided by the PROOF Plane's Ed25519 digital signatures on every governance decision record. The SHA-256 hash chain provides additional integrity assurance. Chain verification is automated via /v1/proof/{proof_id}/verify.
Continuous evidence for AU-10 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Non-repudiation is provided through Ed25519 digital signatures (app/core/signatures.py). The SignatureManager generates Ed25519 key pairs (128-bit security, 64-byte signatures) and signs each proof record with deterministic serialization (json.dumps with sort_keys=True). The sign_proof_record function creates signatures over the canonical JSON representation of record data. The verify_proof_signature function validates signatures using the public key. Signatures are base64-encoded and stored in the signature field of each ProofRecordDB (app/db/models.py). The public key can be exported via get_public_key_pem() for external verification. Key loading supports file-based (PEM), environment variable (base64), and ephemeral generation modes.
by-components.remarks: Ed25519 signatures provide cryptographic non-repudiation for all proof chain records.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AU-11 (Audit Record Retention) as part of the Audit and Accountability control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AU-11 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Audit record retention is defined in the evidence mapper (app/core/evidence_mapper.py) with framework-specific retention periods: NIST 800-53 (7 years), EU AI Act (10 years), ISO 42001 (7 years), SOC 2 (5 years), NIST AI RMF (7 years), CMMC (7 years), GDPR (6 years). Each ControlEvidence record includes a retention_expires timestamp calculated from the framework's retention policy. The proof chain database uses PostgreSQL in production (Neon serverless) with no automatic purging — records are retained until explicit deletion. The hash chain architecture ensures that record deletion would invalidate chain integrity, effectively enforcing retention through cryptographic constraints.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate defines and enforces framework-specific retention periods. Consumer organization manages database storage capacity for the defined retention periods.

by-components.description: Vorion implements AU-12 (Audit Record Generation) as part of the Audit and Accountability control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for AU-12 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Audit record generation occurs at all three pipeline stages. The INTENT layer (app/routers/intent.py) generates records for intent submissions including tripwire triggers and critic verdicts. The ENFORCE layer (app/routers/enforce.py) generates records for policy evaluation decisions including velocity violations and circuit breaker blocks. The PROOF layer (app/routers/proof.py) generates the immutable proof chain records with hash chains and signatures. The async log queue (app/core/async_logger.py) provides non-blocking audit record generation. The evidence mapper automatically generates compliance evidence records from proof chain events across 7 frameworks. The control health engine (app/core/control_health.py) reports AU-12 as 'compliant' when all three layers (intent, enforce, proof) are active.
by-components.remarks: Audit record generation is automatic and comprehensive across all pipeline layers.

by-components.description: A system-wide, time-correlated audit trail is maintained through the proof chain (app/db/proof_repository.py). All proof records are sequentially ordered by chain_position with UTC timestamps (created_at), enabling time-correlated analysis across the entire system. The hash chain (previous_hash -> hash linkage) establishes cryptographic ordering that cannot be rearranged. The proof query API supports date range filtering (start_date, end_date) for temporal analysis. Structlog timestamps are synchronized across all subsystems using TimeStamper(fmt='iso'). The evidence mapper (app/core/evidence_mapper.py) generates time-stamped compliance evidence from proof events, creating a correlated compliance audit trail.
by-components.remarks: The hash-chained proof ledger provides an inherently time-correlated, system-wide audit trail.

by-components.description: Changes to audit record generation configuration require admin authentication (X-Admin-Key). Policy engine modifications that affect what events are logged require admin access. The compliance monitoring trigger (POST /v1/compliance/monitor/trigger) requires admin authentication. All configuration changes are tracked through structured logging with admin-specific log events (admin_circuit_halt, admin_circuit_reset, etc.). The policy engine supports register_policy/unregister_policy operations which are logged but restricted to programmatic access with appropriate authorization.
by-components.remarks: Audit configuration changes require admin authorization. Consumer organization establishes change management procedures for audit configuration.

by-components.description: Vorion implements CA-1 (Policy and Procedures) as part of the Assessment, Authorization, and Monitoring control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for CA-1 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Assessment, authorization, and monitoring policy is implemented through the compliance monitoring framework (app/routers/compliance.py, app/core/control_health.py). The health endpoint hierarchy (/health, /health/live, /ready) supports Kubernetes liveness and readiness probes for orchestrated recovery.

The health endpoint hierarchy supports orchestrated restarts with /health/live (process alive) and /ready (service ready) probes.
