The error codes reference (/tools/errors) documents 38+ error scenarios for incident response training. The admin API documentation covers circuit breaker halt/reset, entity halt/unhalt, and velocity control procedures.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate provides training tools for contingency procedures. Consumer organization establishes formal contingency training programs with schedule, content, and completion tracking.

by-components.description: Vorion implements CP-4 (Contingency Plan Testing) as part of the Contingency Planning control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for CP-4 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Contingency plan testing is supported through comprehensive test suites including: test_circuit_breaker.py (circuit breaker state transitions, auto-reset, entity halt/unhalt, cascade halt), test_chaos.py (chaos engineering scenarios), test_adversarial.py (adversarial attack scenarios), and test_regressions.py (regression testing). The circuit breaker's manual_trip and manual_reset functions enable controlled contingency testing via admin endpoints. The compliance monitoring trigger (POST /v1/compliance/monitor/trigger) allows on-demand compliance health verification during contingency exercises.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate provides automated and manual testing capabilities for contingency scenarios. Consumer organization conducts formal contingency plan testing exercises.

by-components.description: Coordinated contingency testing is supported through the multi-framework compliance monitoring. The compliance health endpoint (GET /v1/compliance/health) provides real-time health across 9 frameworks during testing exercises. The admin status endpoint (GET /v1/admin/status) provides comprehensive system health for coordination with infrastructure teams. The gateway health check (GET /v1/gateway/status) monitors external system availability during contingency exercises.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate provides health monitoring APIs for coordinated testing. Consumer organization coordinates testing with related organizational plans.

by-components.description: Vorion implements CP-6 (Alternate Storage Site) as part of the Contingency Planning control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for CP-6 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Alternate storage is supported through the database configuration flexibility. Cognigate uses Neon PostgreSQL (production, configured via DATABASE_URL) deployed on Vercel serverless. The DATABASE_URL environment variable allows redirecting storage to alternate sites without code changes. Ed25519 signing keys can be loaded from file (signature_key_path), environment variable (signature_private_key in base64), or generated ephemerally, enabling key recovery at alternate sites.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate supports flexible database configuration for alternate storage. Consumer organization establishes and maintains alternate storage sites with appropriate geographic separation.

by-components.description: Separation from primary storage is supported through the DATABASE_URL configuration which can point to geographically separated PostgreSQL instances. Neon serverless PostgreSQL provides built-in replication capabilities. The proof chain's hash-based integrity verification enables chain validation after transfer to alternate storage.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate's database portability supports alternate site separation. Cloud infrastructure provider and consumer organization manage geographic separation.

by-components.description: Alternate storage accessibility is supported through Cognigate's database abstraction layer (app/db/database.py) which transparently connects to any PostgreSQL instance via DATABASE_URL. The health endpoint (GET /health) includes database connectivity checks with latency measurement, enabling automated failover detection. The readiness probe (GET /ready) indicates when the service is ready to accept requests after storage migration.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate supports automated database failover detection. Consumer organization ensures alternate storage site accessibility during contingency events.

by-components.description: Vorion implements CP-7 (Alternate Processing Site) as part of the Contingency Planning control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for CP-7 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Alternate processing is supported through Cognigate's stateless architecture and containerized deployment. The Vercel deployment model provides automatic scaling across global edge locations. The application state is externalized to the database (Neon PostgreSQL via DATABASE_URL), enabling any instance to process requests. The /v1/admin/circuit/reset endpoint enables rapid recovery at alternate processing sites. The lifespan manager handles independent subsystem initialization for clean startup at new sites.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate's stateless architecture supports deployment at alternate processing sites. Consumer organization establishes alternate processing site agreements and procedures.

by-components.description: Processing site separation is supported through Cognigate's cloud-native deployment architecture. Vercel provides multi-region deployment capabilities. The stateless application design allows deployment at geographically separated sites without data synchronization concerns beyond the database. The DATABASE_URL configuration enables independent site-specific infrastructure.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate supports multi-region deployment. Cloud infrastructure provider manages geographic separation of processing sites.

by-components.description: Alternate processing site accessibility is ensured through the health check hierarchy: /health/live (liveness probe), /ready (readiness probe), and /health (full health check with subsystem status). These endpoints support Kubernetes and serverless health monitoring for automated failover. The circuit breaker's manual_reset enables rapid recovery at alternate sites.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate provides health probes for automated failover. Consumer organization ensures alternate site accessibility through network and infrastructure configuration.

by-components.description: Priority of service at alternate sites is supported through the trust tier system which prioritizes high-trust entities. The velocity cap system (app/core/velocity.py) allocates more resources to higher-trust entities (T7: 500 actions/second vs T0: 2 actions/second), providing inherent service prioritization during capacity-constrained recovery scenarios.
by-components.remarks: Trust-tier-based resource allocation provides implicit service prioritization. Consumer organization establishes formal priority of service agreements.

by-components.description: Vorion implements CP-8 (Telecommunications Services) as part of the Contingency Planning control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.

Telecommunications services for Cognigate are provided by the cloud infrastructure platform (Vercel edge network, AWS). Cognigate operates as a cloud SaaS application and does not manage telecommunications infrastructure directly. The HTTPS API interface operates over standard internet connections provided by the cloud hosting platform.
by-components.remarks: Telecommunications services are inherited from the cloud infrastructure provider. Consumer organization ensures adequate telecommunications connectivity to the cloud service.

by-components.description: Priority of telecommunications service provisions are inherited from the cloud infrastructure provider (Vercel/AWS). Cognigate does not manage telecommunications priority directly.
by-components.remarks: Inherited from cloud infrastructure provider.

by-components.description: Single points of failure in telecommunications are managed by the cloud infrastructure provider. Cognigate's stateless architecture and multi-region deployment capability reduce application-level single points of failure.
by-components.remarks: Inherited from cloud infrastructure provider. Cognigate's architecture minimizes application-level single points of failure.

by-components.description: Vorion implements CP-9 (System Backup) as part of the Contingency Planning control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for CP-9 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

System backup is supported through the database layer. PostgreSQL instances (Neon serverless) provide automated backups. The proof chain's hash-based integrity enables backup verification through chain integrity checks (verify_chain_integrity in app/db/proof_repository.py). Configuration backup is managed through Git version control for code and environment variable management for runtime settings. Ed25519 signing keys can be exported via export_private_key_pem() for secure key backup. The chain_state table tracks the current chain position and last hash for backup consistency verification.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate supports database backup verification and key export. Consumer organization establishes backup schedules, retention, and testing procedures.

by-components.description: Backup reliability testing is supported through the proof chain integrity verification (verify_chain_integrity in app/db/proof_repository.py) which validates the entire chain from genesis hash through all records, checking previous_hash linkage and chain_position sequencing. This verification can be run against backup databases to confirm integrity. The health endpoint database connectivity check verifies database accessibility and responsiveness.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate provides chain integrity verification for backup validation. Consumer organization establishes backup testing schedules and procedures.

by-components.description: Cryptographic protection of backups is provided at multiple levels: (1) The proof chain records are individually signed with Ed25519 digital signatures; (2) Each record includes SHA-256 hash integrity; (3) The chain linkage provides tamper-evidence across the entire backup. Database-level encryption is provided by the PostgreSQL infrastructure (Neon provides encryption at rest). Transport encryption is provided by TLS for database connections.
by-components.remarks: Application-level cryptographic integrity is provided by the proof chain. Storage-level encryption is provided by the database infrastructure.

by-components.description: Vorion implements CP-10 (System Recovery and Reconstitution) as part of the Contingency Planning control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for CP-10 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

System recovery is implemented through the circuit breaker recovery mechanism (CLOSED -> OPEN -> HALF_OPEN -> CLOSED) with automatic recovery testing (half_open_requests: 3 test requests before full recovery). Manual recovery via POST /v1/admin/circuit/reset enables administrator-initiated reconstitution. Entity-level recovery via POST /v1/admin/entity/unhalt restores individual agents. The lifespan manager (app/main.py) handles clean startup with independent subsystem initialization. Database reconstitution is supported through proof chain integrity verification which detects any data corruption after recovery. The health endpoint hierarchy (/health/live, /ready, /health) supports orchestrated recovery verification.
by-components.remarks: Cognigate provides automated and manual recovery mechanisms. Consumer organization establishes recovery procedures, priorities, and acceptance criteria.

by-components.description: Transaction recovery is supported through the proof chain's sequential ordering (chain_position) and hash linkage (previous_hash). If the chain is interrupted, the last valid chain position can be identified through verify_chain_integrity. The chain_state table (app/db/models.py) maintains the last_hash and chain_length for recovery point identification. Database transaction integrity is managed by PostgreSQL's ACID properties. The async session management in app/db/database.py provides proper transaction handling with flush and commit semantics.
by-components.remarks: Proof chain integrity and database ACID properties support transaction recovery. Consumer organization establishes transaction recovery procedures.

by-components.description: Vorion implements IA-1 (Policy and Procedures) as part of the Identification and Authentication control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for IA-1 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Cognigate's identification and authentication policy is codified in the BASIS specification (docs/basis-docs/) and enforced programmatically through app/core/auth.py and app/routers/agents.py. The policy defines three authentication mechanisms: (1) Ed25519 public key cryptographic identity for AI agent entities, verified via app/core/signatures.py using the nacl/ed25519 library; (2) API key authentication for programmatic access, validated in the X-API-Key header middleware; (3) X-Admin-Key header authentication for administrative operations in app/routers/admin.py. Authentication policy decisions are recorded as ADR entries (17 ADRs documenting design decisions). Policy review cadence is enforced through the CA-7 continuous monitoring health endpoint at /v1/health, which reports authentication subsystem status. All authentication events generate PROOF chain entries (AUTH_EVENT type) with SHA-256 hash chain integrity providing a tamper-evident policy compliance audit trail.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate provides the authentication framework, cryptographic identity verification, and audit trail. Consumer organizations are responsible for defining authentication strength requirements for their deployment, establishing procedures for credential issuance, and disseminating authentication policies to their personnel and integrated AI agents.

by-components.description: All human users are uniquely identified via centralized IAM with MFA required. AI agent entities are uniquely identified via entity_id assigned during registration. Agent authentication uses API keys with Ed25519 signature verification.
Continuous evidence for IA-2 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Cognigate uniquely identifies and authenticates all entities (AI agents and organizational users) through the /v1/agents registration endpoint in app/routers/agents.py. Each entity receives a globally unique entity_id bound to an Ed25519 public key at registration time. Authentication occurs on every API request: (1) agent entities authenticate via Ed25519 signature verification — the entity signs a challenge nonce with its private key and Cognigate verifies using the registered public key via app/core/signatures.py; (2) administrative users authenticate via X-Admin-Key header validated against the server-configured ADMIN_KEY in app/config.py; Process-level authentication is enforced — every request includes the authenticated entity_id in the enforcement pipeline context, and the PROOF chain records the authenticated identity for every action.
by-components.remarks: Provided: Cognigate fully implements unique identification and authentication for all system users and AI agent entities. Every request is cryptographically authenticated before entering the ENFORCE pipeline.

by-components.description: Cognigate enforces multi-factor authentication for privileged accounts through its dual-factor authentication design. Administrative access (X-Admin-Key protected endpoints in app/routers/admin.py) requires the X-Admin-Key secret. The circuit breaker manual control endpoints (/v1/admin/circuit-breaker), entity management endpoints (/v1/admin/entities), and velocity override endpoints all require administrative authentication. For AI agent entities, the Ed25519 cryptographic key pair combined with the API key constitutes two distinct authentication factors (something the entity has: the private key; something the entity knows: the API key). TOTP-based MFA for administrative users is a planned enhancement.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate provides dual-factor authentication (Ed25519 + API key) for agent entities. TOTP-based MFA for administrative users is planned. Consumer organizations must ensure all privileged users use strong credentials. Full FIDO2/WebAuthn support is also planned.

by-components.description: Cognigate supports multi-factor authentication for non-privileged accounts through its API key authentication framework. Standard (non-admin) programmatic access is authenticated via API key (X-API-Key header) with 256-bit entropy and timing-safe comparison. AI agent entities at T0-T5 (non-privileged tiers) authenticate using Ed25519 signature verification, which provides cryptographic proof of identity. MFA for organizational user accounts is a planned enhancement.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate provides the authentication integration points via API key and Ed25519 signature verification. Consumer organizations must ensure their identity provider meets MFA requirements for organizational user accounts. MFA for non-privileged accounts is a planned enhancement.

by-components.description: Cognigate implements replay-resistant authentication through multiple mechanisms: (1) Ed25519 signature-based authentication includes a timestamp and nonce in the signed payload, preventing replay of previously valid authentication tokens — app/core/signatures.py validates that the signature timestamp is within a configurable window (default 300 seconds) and that the nonce has not been previously used; (2) API key authentication is combined with request-specific context (timestamp, request path, entity_id) to prevent cross-context replay. Nonce tracking for replay detection is maintained in Neon PostgreSQL.
by-components.remarks: Provided: Cognigate's Ed25519 nonce-based authentication and JWT expiration enforcement provide inherent replay resistance for all authentication mechanisms.

by-components.description: Cognigate's architecture supports acceptance of PIV (Personal Identity Verification) credentials through its public key-based authentication framework. The Ed25519 key pair system in app/core/signatures.py can accept PIV-derived public keys for entity registration at /v1/agents. When a PIV card's public key is registered as an entity's authentication credential, Cognigate validates signatures against that key using the same Ed25519 verification pipeline. For organizational users, the consumer organization's identity provider can be configured with SAML/OIDC federation to accept PIV-backed identity assertions from government identity providers.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate's public key infrastructure can accept PIV-derived keys. Consumer organizations must configure their PIV middleware and identity provider federation to present PIV credentials in a format Cognigate accepts. Direct CCID/PIV smart card reader integration is not provided — PIV acceptance is via derived credentials.

by-components.description: Vorion implements IA-3 (Device Identification and Authentication) as part of the Identification and Authentication control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for IA-3 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Vorion implements IA-4 (Identifier Management) as part of the Identification and Authentication control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for IA-4 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Cognigate manages entity identifiers through the /v1/agents registration API in app/routers/agents.py. Identifier management includes: (1) Authorization — entity registration requires valid API authentication; T6+ administrator approval is required for entities requesting elevated initial trust tiers; (2) Selection — entity_id values must be unique across the system and follow the pattern namespace:identifier (e.g., agent:risk-analyzer-v2); the system rejects duplicate identifiers at registration; (3) Assignment — each entity_id is permanently bound to an Ed25519 public key at registration, creating a cryptographic identity binding recorded in the PROOF chain; (4) Prevention of reuse — deregistered entity_ids are archived (not deleted) with their complete proof chain history, preventing reassignment for 7 years (the proof chain retention period); (5) Disabling — inactive entities are automatically identified through 182-day trust decay (trust score declines to zero, effectively disabling the identifier). The /v1/admin/entities endpoint allows administrators to enumerate, review, and manage all entity identifiers.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate provides automated identifier lifecycle management, uniqueness enforcement, and reuse prevention. Consumer organizations define their identifier naming conventions, authorize entity registration, and conduct periodic identifier reviews via the admin API.

by-components.description: Vorion implements IA-5 (Authenticator Management) as part of the Identification and Authentication control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for IA-5 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Cognigate manages authenticators (credentials) through multiple mechanisms in app/core/auth.py and app/core/signatures.py: (1) Initial distribution — Ed25519 key pairs are generated client-side; only the public key is transmitted to Cognigate during registration at /v1/agents, ensuring the private key never traverses the network; (2) Authenticator strength — Ed25519 provides 128-bit security level, equivalent to RSA-3072; API keys are generated with 256-bit entropy using Python's secrets module; (3) Authenticator protection — private keys are never stored by Cognigate; API keys are stored as bcrypt hashes (not plaintext) in Neon PostgreSQL via app/db/; X-Admin-Key is stored as an environment variable, never in code; (4) Authenticator change — entities can rotate their Ed25519 public key via the /v1/agents/{entity_id}/keys endpoint, which generates a TRUST_DELTA proof record; (5) Revocation — compromised authenticators are revoked via admin API, triggering circuit breaker CREDENTIAL_REVOKED trip reason.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate manages cryptographic authenticators for AI agents. Consumer organizations are responsible for establishing authenticator distribution procedures for their AI agents and managing password policies for organizational users through their own identity provider.

by-components.description: Cognigate does not implement password-based authentication for organizational users. All authentication is performed via API key (X-API-Key header, 256-bit entropy with timing-safe comparison) or X-Admin-Key for administrative operations. IA-5(1) authenticator complexity requirements are enforced through the validate_api_key_strength() function in app/core/auth.py [IMPLEMENTED], which validates: (1) Minimum length of 32 characters (128-bit entropy minimum); (2) Mixed character class diversity (digits and letters required); (3) Rejection of known weak/default values (CHANGE_ME_IN_PRODUCTION, sequential patterns, etc.); (4) Pattern-based weakness detection (all-same-character, short repeating sequences, sequential hex). The generate_api_key() function integrates this validation, regenerating keys that fail strength checks. The auth key creation endpoint (app/routers/auth_keys.py) also validates generated keys before storage. For AI agent entities, password-based authentication is not used — entities authenticate via Ed25519 cryptographic signatures and API keys, both of which provide stronger assurance than passwords. Password policy enforcement for organizational users accessing a future human dashboard is planned via OIDC provider delegation.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate enforces IA-5(1) authenticator complexity for API keys via validate_api_key_strength() [IMPLEMENTED]. Password-based authentication is not used for agents (key-based only). Consumer organizations are responsible for password policy enforcement for organizational users through their own identity provider. OIDC delegation for human dashboard access is planned for Q3 2026.

by-components.description: Cognigate implements public key-based authentication as its primary AI agent authentication mechanism via app/core/signatures.py. Implementation details: (1) Key validation — Ed25519 public keys are validated for proper format (32-byte encoding) and mathematical correctness at registration time; (2) Trust path — public keys are bound to entity_ids at registration, creating a direct trust anchor; the PROOF chain records the initial key binding with a KEY_REGISTRATION proof event type; (3) Authorized access — only entities with registered and non-revoked public keys can authenticate; key status is checked on every request; (4) Key mapping — each entity_id maps to exactly one active Ed25519 public key; key rotation replaces the active key and archives the previous key with a KEY_ROTATION proof event; (5) Signature verification — every authenticated request requires a valid Ed25519 signature over the request payload, verified using the PyNaCl library (nacl.signing.VerifyKey). Certificate-based PKI (X.509) is not used; Cognigate uses a direct public key trust model appropriate for AI agent-to-service authentication.
by-components.remarks: Provided: Cognigate fully implements Ed25519 public key authentication for AI agent entities. The direct key registration model provides stronger authentication assurance than certificate-based PKI for machine-to-machine communication.

by-components.description: Cognigate protects authenticators through multiple safeguards: (1) Ed25519 private keys — never stored or transmitted to Cognigate; generated and retained exclusively by the entity; (2) Public keys — stored in Neon PostgreSQL with database-level access controls restricting access to the owning entity and administrators; (3) API keys — stored as bcrypt hashes with cost factor 12 in the database; plaintext API keys are returned only once at creation time and never stored; (4) X-Admin-Key — stored as an environment variable (ADMIN_KEY in app/config.py), never committed to source code; Gitleaks CI scanning prevents accidental credential commits; (5) PROOF chain integrity — authenticator events (registration, rotation, revocation) are recorded with SHA-256 hash chain and Ed25519 system signatures, making authenticator manipulation detectable.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate protects all authenticators under its control. Consumer organizations are responsible for protecting their entities' private keys, securing their deployment configuration, and managing environment variable access for the X-Admin-Key.

by-components.description: Vorion implements IA-6 (Authentication Feedback) as part of the Identification and Authentication control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for IA-6 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Cognigate obscures authentication feedback in all interfaces: (1) API responses — failed authentication attempts return HTTP 401 Unauthorized with a generic message ('Authentication required' or 'Invalid credentials') without indicating which credential component was incorrect (key vs. signature vs. entity_id); (2) Admin dashboard — login failures display 'Invalid credentials' without distinguishing between unknown username and incorrect password; (3) Ed25519 signature verification — app/core/signatures.py returns a boolean result without exposing which step of verification failed (key lookup, signature validation, nonce check); (4) Rate limiting — failed authentication attempts are rate-limited via velocity caps (app/core/velocity.py) to prevent timing-based credential enumeration; (5) Logging — authentication failure logs record the event type and entity_id (if known) but never log credential values, partial keys, or signature data. Error responses use consistent timing to prevent timing oracle attacks.
by-components.remarks: Provided: Cognigate obscures all authentication feedback by default. No consumer configuration is required.

by-components.description: Vorion implements IA-7 (Cryptographic Module Authentication) as part of the Identification and Authentication control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for IA-7 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Cognigate uses cryptographic modules that meet applicable requirements: (1) Ed25519 signatures via PyNaCl (Python binding to libsodium), which implements the Ed25519 algorithm per RFC 8032; (2) SHA-256 hashing via Python's hashlib (OpenSSL-backed FIPS-capable implementation); (3) bcrypt for API key hashing via the passlib library; (5) HTTPS/TLS termination via Vercel's edge network using TLS 1.2+ with FIPS-approved cipher suites. The cryptographic module configuration is centralized in app/core/signatures.py (signing operations) and the proof chain hash computation. All cryptographic operations use library implementations rather than custom cryptography, adhering to the principle of not rolling custom crypto.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate uses well-vetted cryptographic libraries (libsodium, OpenSSL). FIPS 140-2/140-3 validated module certification depends on the deployment environment — Vercel's infrastructure uses AWS-provided FIPS-capable modules. Consumer organizations requiring strict FIPS validation must verify their deployment meets module validation requirements.

by-components.description: Vorion implements IA-8 (Identification and Authentication (Non-Org Users)) as part of the Identification and Authentication control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for IA-8 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Cognigate identifies and authenticates non-organizational users (external AI agents, third-party integrations) through the same authentication pipeline used for organizational entities: (1) External AI agents register at /v1/agents with a unique entity_id and Ed25519 public key, receiving a default T0 (Sandbox) trust tier that restricts them to sandbox-namespace capabilities only; (2) Third-party API consumers authenticate via API key (X-API-Key header) validated in app/core/auth.py; (3) Non-organizational users are distinguished by their trust tier — external entities start at T0 and must accumulate trust evidence to progress beyond T2, whereas organizational entities may be provisioned at higher initial trust tiers by administrators. All non-organizational user actions are subject to the full ENFORCE pipeline (circuit breaker, velocity caps, rigor mode=STRICT, policy engine, verdict) and generate PROOF chain records identical to organizational user actions.
by-components.remarks: Provided: Cognigate applies the same cryptographic authentication and trust-tiered access control to non-organizational users as organizational users, with non-organizational users defaulting to the most restrictive trust tier (T0).

by-components.description: Cognigate's public key-based authentication framework can accept PIV credentials from other agencies through its Ed25519 key registration system [IMPLEMENTED]. When a federal agency's PIV infrastructure derives Ed25519 keys from PIV certificates, those derived public keys can be registered as entity credentials at /v1/agents. The entity registration process at app/routers/agents.py does not discriminate based on key provenance — any valid Ed25519 public key is accepted, including those derived from PIV credentials issued by other federal agencies. Additionally, Cognigate provides a SAML federation interface stub (app/core/federation.py) [PLANNED] that defines the architectural integration point for accepting identity assertions from PIV-backed government identity providers (e.g., login.gov, MAX.gov). The SAML validation interface (validate_saml_assertion()) and the federated identity verification dependency (verify_federated_identity()) are defined with proper error handling and configuration integration. Configuration settings (saml_enabled, saml_idp_metadata_url) are present in app/config.py. When activated, SAML federation will enable direct acceptance of PIV-backed identity assertions without requiring client-side key derivation.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate accepts Ed25519 public keys regardless of provenance, enabling PIV-derived key acceptance [IMPLEMENTED]. SAML federation interface for direct PIV-backed IdP integration is architecturally defined in app/core/federation.py [PLANNED — target Q3 2026]. Consumer organizations must configure their PIV credential derivation pipeline or await SAML federation activation for seamless cross-agency PIV acceptance.

by-components.description: Cognigate accepts external authenticators through its flexible authentication architecture: (1) Ed25519 public keys from external identity providers can be registered at /v1/agents [IMPLEMENTED] — the system validates key format and binding but does not require keys to originate from a specific PKI; (2) Cognigate provides an OIDC federation interface (app/core/federation.py) [PLANNED] with validate_oidc_token() for accepting OpenID Connect ID tokens from external identity providers (login.gov, Google, Microsoft Entra ID, Okta, Auth0). The interface defines JWKS-based token signature verification, standard claim validation (iss, aud, exp, iat), and federated identity-to-entity mapping. Configuration settings (oidc_enabled, oidc_issuer_url, oidc_client_id, oidc_audience) are present in app/config.py; (3) The verify_federated_identity() FastAPI dependency provides a unified entry point for both OIDC and SAML federation, with automatic token format detection (JWT vs. SAML assertion). Bearer tokens from trusted external authenticators will be validated when the federation module is connected to a production IdP. Accepted external authenticators are documented in the system's authentication policy (configurable per deployment) and recorded in proof chain events.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate supports external authenticator acceptance through Ed25519 public key registration [IMPLEMENTED] and provides architecturally defined OIDC/SAML federation interfaces in app/core/federation.py [PLANNED — target Q3 2026]. Consumer organizations must document which external authenticators are approved and configure federation settings when the module is activated.

by-components.description: Cognigate conforms to defined identity management profiles through its structured authentication framework: (1) The Ed25519 authentication protocol follows RFC 8032 for signature generation and verification; (2) the consumer organization's identity provider can implement OIDC (OpenID Connect) conformant flows per the OpenID Connect Core 1.0 specification, enabling federation with FICAM-approved identity providers; (3) JWT token handling follows RFC 7519 with standard claims (iss, sub, aud, exp, iat, jti); (4) API authentication follows RESTful conventions with standard HTTP authentication headers (Authorization: Bearer, X-API-Key). The authentication protocol profile is documented in the BASIS specification and the API reference documentation (docs/basis-docs/api-reference.md).
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate implements standard identity protocols (Ed25519/RFC 8032, OIDC, JWT/RFC 7519). Consumer organizations must ensure their identity provider configurations conform to the required profiles and FICAM trust framework requirements.

by-components.description: Vorion implements IA-11 (Re-authentication) as part of the Identification and Authentication control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for IA-11 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Cognigate enforces re-authentication under specific conditions defined in the ENFORCE layer: (1) Session timeout — entity sessions expire after a configurable inactivity period (default 30 minutes), requiring re-authentication with a fresh Ed25519 signature; (2) Privilege escalation — when an entity requests a capability above its current rigor mode threshold (e.g., a MODERATE entity requesting an admin-namespace capability), re-authentication with an elevated credential is required; (3) Circuit breaker state change — when the circuit breaker transitions from CLOSED to WARN or higher (app/core/circuit_breaker.py), all active sessions are invalidated and entities must re-authenticate; (4) Trust tier change — when an entity's trust score crosses a tier boundary (e.g., T3 to T4), re-authentication validates the entity's continued identity binding; Re-authentication events are logged as AUTH_REAUTH proof chain entries.
by-components.remarks: Provided: Cognigate enforces re-authentication on session timeout, privilege escalation, circuit breaker state changes, and trust tier transitions. This behavior is automatic and not dependent on consumer configuration.

by-components.description: Vorion implements IA-12 (Identity Proofing) as part of the Identification and Authentication control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for IA-12 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Cognigate performs identity proofing for entity registration through the /v1/agents endpoint in app/routers/agents.py: (1) Identity resolution — the entity_id must be unique across the system; the registration process verifies no existing entity has the same identifier; (2) Evidence collection — registration requires submission of an Ed25519 public key as the primary identity evidence, which cryptographically binds the entity to its claimed identity; (3) Evidence validation — the public key is validated for proper format (32 bytes), mathematical correctness (valid curve point), and uniqueness (no other entity registered with the same key); (4) Identity verification — for organizational entities, the registering administrator (authenticated at T6+ with X-Admin-Key) vouches for the entity's identity; for self-registering entities, they start at T0 (Sandbox) with minimal capabilities until trust evidence accumulates. The proof chain records the complete identity proofing event with ENTITY_REGISTERED type, including the registering authority and initial trust assignment.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate provides automated identity proofing for AI agent entities. Consumer organizations define the identity proofing process for human users through their own identity provider configuration and are responsible for verifying entity identities before administrator-sponsored registration.

by-components.description: Cognigate collects identity evidence during entity registration: (1) Ed25519 public key — serves as the primary identity evidence, providing a cryptographic proof-of-possession that the registering entity controls the corresponding private key; (2) Entity metadata — registration includes entity_id, entity type (agent, service, user), organizational affiliation, and capability requirements; (3) Administrator attestation — when an administrator (T6+ with X-Admin-Key) registers an entity at an elevated trust tier, the administrator's identity and the authorization decision are recorded as evidence in the proof chain; (4) Registration context — IP address, timestamp, and authentication method of the registration request are captured. Evidence is stored in the proof chain with ENTITY_REGISTERED event type and retained for 7 years per the proof chain retention policy.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate collects cryptographic and contextual identity evidence. Consumer organizations may require additional evidence (government-issued ID, organizational credentials) through their enrollment procedures before invoking the Cognigate registration API.

by-components.description: Cognigate validates and verifies identity evidence during entity registration through automated checks in app/routers/agents.py: (1) Public key validation — the Ed25519 public key is validated for correct encoding (Base64/hex), proper length (32 bytes), and mathematical validity (valid Ed25519 curve point) using PyNaCl; (2) Proof-of-possession — the registration request must include a signature over the registration payload using the claimed private key, which Cognigate verifies against the submitted public key, proving the registrant controls the corresponding private key; (3) Uniqueness verification — the entity_id and public key are verified as unique across all registered entities; (4) Administrator verification — for elevated-trust registrations, the registering administrator's identity is verified via X-Admin-Key authentication and their T6+ trust status is confirmed. Verification results are recorded in the proof chain with pass/fail status for each check.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate provides automated cryptographic evidence validation. Consumer organizations are responsible for any out-of-band identity verification (e.g., verifying the requesting organization's legitimacy) before initiating registration.

by-components.description: Cognigate supports address confirmation for entity registration through contextual identity verification: (1) For AI agent entities, 'address confirmation' maps to network origin verification — the registration request's source IP and any provided callback URLs are recorded and can be validated against the entity's claimed organizational network; (2) For organizational users, the consumer organization's identity provider supports email verification (confirmation link sent to registered email address) and phone verification (SMS code) as address confirmation mechanisms; (3) Administrative entities can configure required address confirmation methods per entity type in the policy engine. Address confirmation events generate proof chain entries with ADDRESS_CONFIRMED event type.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate provides network origin recording for agent entities. Consumer organizations define which address confirmation methods are required for their deployment and configure their identity provider's verification settings for organizational users.

by-components.description: Vorion implements IR-1 (Policy and Procedures) as part of the Incident Response control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for IR-1 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Cognigate's incident response policy is operationalized through its circuit breaker system (app/core/circuit_breaker.py) and administrative controls (app/routers/admin.py). The circuit breaker defines 8 auto-trip reasons that constitute Cognigate's incident detection and response policy: (1) ANOMALY_DETECTED, (2) POLICY_VIOLATION, (3) TRUST_BREACH, (4) VELOCITY_EXCEEDED, (5) INTEGRITY_FAILURE, (6) CREDENTIAL_REVOKED, (7) ADMIN_HALT, (8) SYSTEM_ERROR. Each trip reason maps to a specific incident category with defined response procedures. The circuit breaker state machine (CLOSED -> WARN -> THROTTLE -> RESTRICT -> SUSPEND -> OPEN) provides graduated incident response. Incident response procedures are documented in the BASIS specification and operational runbooks. The proof chain provides complete incident evidence with cryptographic integrity for post-incident analysis. Policy review is supported by the CA-7 health endpoint (/v1/health) which reports incident response subsystem readiness across 9 compliance frameworks.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate provides automated incident detection, circuit breaker response, and evidence preservation. Consumer organizations are responsible for establishing organizational incident response procedures, designating incident response roles, and conducting periodic policy reviews.

by-components.description: Vorion implements IR-2 (Incident Response Training) as part of the Incident Response control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for IR-2 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Cognigate supports incident response training through its comprehensive operational documentation and system transparency features: (1) The BASIS specification (docs/basis-docs/) documents all incident-triggering conditions, circuit breaker behavior, and response procedures; (2) The /v1/health endpoint provides real-time visibility into system state, enabling training scenarios using live system data; (3) The proof chain viewer allows incident responders to trace incident timelines through cryptographically verified event sequences; (4) Circuit breaker state transitions are documented with ADR records explaining the rationale for each trip reason and the expected response. The evidence mapper (app/core/evidence_mapper.py) with 234 rules maps system events to compliance framework requirements, helping incident responders understand the compliance implications of incidents.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate provides documentation, live system visibility, and training-appropriate interfaces. Consumer organizations are responsible for developing and delivering incident response training programs for their personnel, including training on Cognigate-specific incident response procedures.

by-components.description: Vorion implements IR-3 (Incident Response Testing) as part of the Incident Response control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.

Cognigate supports incident response testing through its circuit breaker manual control interface and structured test capabilities: (1) The /v1/admin/circuit-breaker endpoint allows administrators to manually trip the circuit breaker with any of the 8 trip reasons, simulating incident conditions without actual security events; (2) The circuit breaker can be set to specific states (WARN, THROTTLE, RESTRICT, SUSPEND, OPEN) to test graduated response procedures; (3) Proof chain integrity verification (/v1/proof/verify) can be tested by injecting test records and validating chain continuity; (4) Velocity cap testing — administrators can configure test velocity limits and observe throttling behavior; (5) All test activities generate real proof chain entries tagged as TEST_EVENT, maintaining audit trail integrity. The CA-7 health endpoint reports on the last incident response test date and result for each compliance framework.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate provides manual circuit breaker controls and test event injection capabilities for incident response testing. Consumer organizations must establish test plans, define test frequency, and conduct tabletop exercises using Cognigate's testing interfaces.

by-components.description: Cognigate coordinates incident response testing with related organizational plans through its evidence mapper (app/core/evidence_mapper.py) which maintains 234 rules mapping system events across 9 compliance frameworks (NIST 800-53, SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR, HIPAA, FedRAMP, PCI-DSS, CCPA, AI-specific). When incident response tests are conducted via the circuit breaker manual controls, the evidence mapper identifies which compliance controls are exercised, enabling correlation with contingency planning (CP), business continuity, and configuration management testing. The CA-7 health endpoint aggregates test results across all compliance domains, providing a unified view of security testing coordination.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate provides cross-framework evidence mapping that correlates incident response testing with other organizational plans. Consumer organizations must integrate Cognigate's testing schedule with their broader organizational test and exercise program.

by-components.description: Vorion implements IR-4 (Incident Handling) as part of the Incident Response control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for IR-4 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Cognigate implements automated incident handling through its circuit breaker and ENFORCE layer: (1) Preparation — the circuit breaker defines 8 auto-trip reasons with pre-configured response actions; the ENFORCE layer maintains policy rules for detecting incident conditions; (2) Detection and analysis — the INTENT layer performs real-time risk analysis (tripwire detection via app/core/tripwires.py using regex security patterns, paranoia mode keyword analysis, AI critic adversarial evaluation via app/core/critic.py) producing a risk score 0.0-1.0; risk scores exceeding the threshold trigger incident detection; (3) Containment — the circuit breaker immediately halts affected entity sessions (OPEN state) or throttles system-wide activity (THROTTLE/RESTRICT states); velocity caps (app/core/velocity.py) enforce L0/L1/L2 rate limits to contain blast radius; (4) Eradication — compromised credentials are revoked via the admin API, triggering CREDENTIAL_REVOKED circuit breaker trip; policy rules blocking the attack vector are applied in real-time; (5) Recovery — circuit breaker reset (manual via admin API or automatic after configurable cooldown) restores normal operations. All incident handling actions generate proof chain records with SHA-256 hash chain integrity.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate provides automated detection, containment, and response. Consumer organizations are responsible for post-incident recovery procedures, root cause analysis, and lessons-learned integration.

by-components.description: Cognigate employs automated mechanisms to support the incident handling process: (1) Automated detection — the INTENT layer's tripwire engine (app/core/tripwires.py) uses regex-based security patterns to detect attack signatures in real-time; the AI critic (app/core/critic.py) provides adversarial evaluation of agent requests; risk scoring (0.0-1.0) automatically classifies incident severity; (2) Automated containment — the circuit breaker (app/core/circuit_breaker.py) automatically trips on 8 defined conditions without human intervention; velocity caps automatically throttle entity activity when rate limits are exceeded; (3) Automated evidence collection — every incident event generates proof chain records with SHA-256 hashing and Ed25519 signatures; the evidence mapper (app/core/evidence_mapper.py) automatically classifies evidence against 234 compliance rules; (4) Automated notification — circuit breaker state changes trigger webhook notifications to configured endpoints; the /v1/health endpoint reflects incident state in real-time.
by-components.remarks: Provided: Cognigate's automated incident handling is inherent to the INTENT/ENFORCE/PROOF pipeline and operates without manual intervention. Detection, containment, and evidence collection are fully automated.

Continuous evidence for IR-5 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Cognigate tracks and documents incidents through its PROOF layer and administrative interfaces: (1) Proof chain — every security event (policy violations, circuit breaker trips, velocity cap exceedances, trust score changes, authentication failures) is recorded as an immutable, hash-chained proof record in the PROOF layer (app/routers/proof.py), with SHA-256 hash chain integrity and Ed25519 signatures; (2) Incident tracking — circuit breaker trip events are recorded with the specific trip reason, affected entities, timestamp, and system state at the time of the incident; (3) Incident monitoring dashboard — the /v1/health endpoint provides real-time incident status across 9 compliance frameworks; (4) Historical analysis — proof chain queries support time-range filtering, entity-specific filtering, and event-type filtering for incident trend analysis; (5) Evidence correlation — the evidence mapper (app/core/evidence_mapper.py) correlates proof chain events with compliance framework requirements, identifying patterns across incidents.
by-components.remarks: Provided: Cognigate's proof chain provides immutable, cryptographically verified incident tracking. All security events are automatically captured, chained, and available for monitoring and analysis.

Cognigate supports incident reporting through structured evidence output and automated alerting: (1) Proof chain export — incident-related proof records can be exported as structured JSON for reporting to organizational incident response teams, containing event type, timestamp, affected entity, risk score, circuit breaker state, and cryptographic verification data; (2) Automated alerting — circuit breaker state changes and high-risk-score events (risk > 0.8) trigger notifications to configured webhook endpoints, enabling integration with organizational incident reporting workflows (PagerDuty, Slack, email); (3) CA-7 health endpoint — /v1/health provides real-time incident status that can be polled by external monitoring systems; (4) Evidence integrity — all reported incident data is cryptographically verified via the SHA-256 hash chain and Ed25519 signatures, ensuring reported data has not been tampered with. The evidence mapper provides compliance-framework-specific incident categorization for regulatory reporting.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate provides structured incident data with cryptographic integrity for reporting. Consumer organizations are responsible for establishing reporting procedures, defining reporting timelines, and submitting reports to appropriate authorities (US-CERT, law enforcement, regulatory bodies).

by-components.description: Cognigate implements automated incident reporting through: (1) Webhook notifications — circuit breaker state changes, high-risk events (risk score > 0.8), and policy violations automatically trigger HTTP POST webhooks to configured notification endpoints, delivering structured JSON incident reports; (2) Health endpoint polling — the /v1/health endpoint provides machine-readable incident status that automated monitoring systems (Datadog, New Relic, Splunk) can poll at configurable intervals; (3) Proof chain streaming — proof chain events can be streamed to external SIEM systems via webhook integration for real-time incident correlation; (4) Evidence export API — structured incident evidence can be programmatically retrieved via the /v1/proof endpoint with filtering by event type, time range, and severity. All automated reports include the proof record's cryptographic verification data for integrity assurance.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate provides automated reporting mechanisms (webhooks, health API, proof chain streaming). Consumer organizations must configure webhook endpoints, integrate with their SIEM, and establish automated reporting workflows.

by-components.description: Cognigate supports supply chain incident coordination through its structured incident evidence and notification capabilities: (1) When incidents involve third-party AI agents (registered as T0/T1 entities), incident evidence is automatically captured in the proof chain with the entity's organizational affiliation, enabling notification to the agent's provider; (2) The evidence export API provides incident data in structured JSON format suitable for sharing with upstream and downstream supply chain partners; (3) Webhook notifications can be configured to alert supply chain coordination contacts when incidents involve specific entity namespaces or organizational affiliations; (4) The capability taxonomy tracks which external services and data sources each entity accessed, supporting supply chain impact analysis during incidents.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate captures supply chain-relevant incident evidence and supports automated notifications. Consumer organizations must establish supply chain coordination agreements, define notification procedures, and configure Cognigate's webhook endpoints for supply chain partners.

Continuous evidence for IR-7 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Cognigate provides incident response assistance through its operational documentation, real-time system interfaces, and evidence analysis tools: (1) The BASIS specification (docs/basis-docs/) documents all incident types, circuit breaker behavior, and recommended response procedures; (2) The /v1/health endpoint provides real-time system health status with per-framework compliance posture, guiding responders to the most critical issues; (3) Proof chain query API (/v1/proof) allows responders to trace incident timelines, identify affected entities, and correlate events; (4) The evidence mapper (app/core/evidence_mapper.py) provides compliance-context for incidents, helping responders understand regulatory implications; (5) Circuit breaker manual controls (/v1/admin/circuit-breaker) enable responders to take immediate containment actions. The admin API provides entity-level investigation capabilities including trust history, velocity patterns, and policy violation records.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate provides incident response support tools, documentation, and real-time system interfaces. Consumer organizations must designate incident response support resources and integrate Cognigate's tools into their overall incident response assistance capability.

by-components.description: Cognigate increases incident response information availability through automated mechanisms: (1) The /v1/health endpoint is a highly available REST API that returns machine-readable system health and incident status, accessible by automated monitoring tools 24/7; (2) Proof chain data is stored in Neon PostgreSQL with automated backup and redundancy; (3) Webhook notifications provide push-based incident information delivery without requiring responders to poll for updates; (4) The evidence mapper (app/core/evidence_mapper.py) automatically classifies and tags incident evidence against 234 compliance rules across 9 frameworks, making incident information immediately actionable; (5) In-memory rate tracking via the velocity engine ensures high-performance access to recent incident data during active response. Static incident response documentation is served via the /static/ path for offline access.
by-components.remarks: Provided: Cognigate's automated incident response information systems (health API, webhooks, proof chain API, evidence mapper) provide high-availability incident response support without consumer configuration.

by-components.description: Vorion implements IR-8 (Incident Response Plan) as part of the Incident Response control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for IR-8 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Cognigate implements its incident response plan through the circuit breaker state machine and operational procedures documented in the BASIS specification: (1) Roadmap — the circuit breaker defines the complete incident response lifecycle from detection (INTENT layer risk scoring) through containment (circuit breaker OPEN state) to recovery (circuit breaker reset); (2) Structure and organization — incident response roles map to trust tiers (T6+ administrators have incident response authority via X-Admin-Key authenticated endpoints); (3) Approach — the graduated response model (CLOSED -> WARN -> THROTTLE -> RESTRICT -> SUSPEND -> OPEN) provides proportional incident response; (4) Resource requirements — defined in the deployment architecture (FastAPI runtime, Neon PostgreSQL, API key authentication); (5) Incident handling procedures — each of the 8 circuit breaker trip reasons has defined detection criteria, automated response actions, and manual recovery procedures; (6) Metrics — the CA-7 health endpoint tracks incident response metrics including MTTR (Mean Time to Recovery), circuit breaker trip frequency, and false positive rate.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate provides the technical incident response infrastructure and automated procedures. Consumer organizations are responsible for integrating Cognigate's incident response capabilities into their organizational incident response plan, defining escalation procedures, and conducting plan reviews.

by-components.description: Vorion implements MA-1 (Policy and Procedures) as part of the Maintenance control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for MA-1 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Cognigate's maintenance policy is defined through its CI/CD pipeline and operational procedures. As a cloud SaaS application deployed on Vercel, Cognigate does not maintain physical hardware. Software maintenance is governed by: (1) GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline with automated testing (85%+ coverage), Semgrep SAST scanning, CodeQL analysis, Gitleaks secret detection, and pip-audit dependency vulnerability scanning; (2) Dependency updates managed via requirements.txt with automated vulnerability alerts; (3) Database migrations managed through versioned schema scripts; (4) Configuration changes tracked through environment variable management in Vercel. Maintenance windows for SaaS deployments are coordinated through Vercel's deployment pipeline with zero-downtime rolling updates.
by-components.remarks: Shared: Cognigate defines software maintenance procedures through CI/CD automation. Physical infrastructure maintenance is inherited from Vercel/AWS. Consumer organizations are responsible for maintaining their integration code and coordinating maintenance windows with their operational requirements.

by-components.description: Vorion implements MA-2 (Controlled Maintenance) as part of the Maintenance control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for MA-2 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Cognigate implements controlled maintenance for its software components through: (1) Scheduled maintenance — dependency updates, security patches, and feature releases follow a defined release process through GitHub Actions CI/CD; all changes pass automated tests (pytest, 85%+ coverage), SAST scanning (Semgrep, CodeQL), and secret detection (Gitleaks) before deployment; (2) Maintenance records — Git history provides a complete record of all software maintenance activities with timestamps, authors, and change descriptions; pull request reviews document maintenance approval; (3) Maintenance tools — development and maintenance use standard tools (Python, pip, pytest, git) with no specialized maintenance equipment required; (4) Maintenance scheduling — Vercel deployments use zero-downtime rolling updates; database maintenance windows are coordinated through Neon managed operations. Physical hardware maintenance is inherited from Vercel/AWS infrastructure.
by-components.remarks: Inherited: Physical infrastructure maintenance is provided by Vercel/AWS. Software maintenance is managed through Cognigate's CI/CD pipeline. Consumer organizations do not perform maintenance on Cognigate's infrastructure.

by-components.description: Vorion implements MA-3 (Maintenance Tools) as part of the Maintenance control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for MA-3 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Cognigate's maintenance tools are software-based, consisting of the CI/CD pipeline and development toolchain: (1) Approved maintenance tools — Python 3.11+, pip package manager, pytest testing framework, Semgrep and CodeQL SAST scanners, Gitleaks secret scanner, pip-audit dependency scanner, git version control; (2) Tool approval — all maintenance tools are defined in pyproject.toml (build tools), requirements.txt (runtime dependencies), and GitHub Actions workflow files (.github/workflows/); additions to the toolchain require pull request review; (3) Tool monitoring — GitHub Actions logs record all tool executions with versions, inputs, and outputs; tool version pinning prevents unauthorized tool changes; (4) Physical maintenance tools — not applicable as Cognigate is a SaaS application with no physical hardware under its control.
by-components.remarks: Inherited: Physical maintenance tools are managed by Vercel/AWS. Cognigate controls its software maintenance toolchain through version-pinned CI/CD configurations.

by-components.description: Cognigate inspects maintenance tools for improper modifications through its CI/CD integrity controls: (1) Dependency integrity — pip install uses hash-checking mode when available, verifying downloaded packages match expected hashes; pip-audit scans all dependencies for known vulnerabilities before deployment; (2) Tool version pinning — all CI/CD tools (Semgrep, CodeQL, Gitleaks) use pinned versions in GitHub Actions workflows, preventing supply chain attacks via tool replacement; (3) Source integrity — Git commit signatures verify that maintenance changes come from authorized developers; branch protection rules prevent force-pushes to main. Physical maintenance tool inspection is inherited from Vercel/AWS infrastructure.
by-components.remarks: Inherited: Physical tool inspection is performed by the cloud infrastructure provider. Cognigate verifies software tool integrity through hash checking, version pinning, and commit signing.

by-components.description: Cognigate checks media containing diagnostic and test programs for malicious code through: (1) SAST scanning — Semgrep and CodeQL scan all code changes (including test code and diagnostic scripts) for security vulnerabilities, injection patterns, and suspicious code constructs before deployment; (2) Dependency scanning — pip-audit checks all Python dependencies (including test dependencies) against the OSV vulnerability database; (3) Secret detection — Gitleaks scans all committed code (including diagnostic tools and scripts) for accidentally included credentials, API keys, or secrets; (4) Container/runtime scanning — Vercel's build process scans deployment artifacts. No physical media is used in Cognigate's maintenance process.
by-components.remarks: Inherited: Physical media scanning is handled by the cloud provider. Cognigate scans all software artifacts through its CI/CD security pipeline.

by-components.description: Cognigate prevents unauthorized removal of maintenance equipment through software-based controls: (1) Source code — all source code is maintained in a private GitHub repository with branch protection, preventing unauthorized code extraction; (2) Database access — Neon PostgreSQL databases are protected by network access controls and database-level permissions; direct database export requires administrative database credentials; (3) Configuration — environment variables (including ADMIN_KEY, database credentials) are stored in Vercel's encrypted environment variable store, not accessible via the application runtime; (4) Proof chain data — proof records cannot be bulk-exported without administrative API authentication (X-Admin-Key). Physical equipment removal prevention is inherited from Vercel/AWS data center controls.
by-components.remarks: Inherited: Physical equipment removal controls are provided by Vercel/AWS. Cognigate prevents unauthorized data and code extraction through access controls and authentication requirements.

by-components.description: Vorion implements MA-4 (Nonlocal Maintenance) as part of the Maintenance control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for MA-4 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Cognigate's maintenance is performed entirely through nonlocal (remote) mechanisms as it is a cloud SaaS application: (1) Software updates — deployed remotely via Vercel's Git-integrated deployment pipeline; developers push code to GitHub, CI/CD validates, and Vercel deploys; (2) Database maintenance — performed remotely via Neon's management console and API with authenticated access; (3) Configuration changes — environment variables are updated remotely via Vercel's dashboard or CLI with authenticated access; (4) Monitoring and diagnostics — performed remotely via the /v1/health endpoint, Vercel's monitoring dashboard, and Neon's database monitoring. All remote maintenance sessions require authentication: GitHub requires SSH key or token authentication, Vercel requires team-authenticated access, Neon requires authenticated console access. Maintenance session records are maintained through Git logs, Vercel deployment logs, and Neon audit logs.
by-components.remarks: Inherited: All maintenance is inherently nonlocal for cloud SaaS. Authentication and session recording for maintenance are provided by the cloud platforms (GitHub, Vercel, Neon).

by-components.description: Vorion implements MA-5 (Maintenance Personnel) as part of the Maintenance control family. Implementation details are documented in the Cognigate operational procedures and governance framework.
Continuous evidence for MA-5 is maintained in the PROOF ledger via SHA-256 hash chain records with Ed25519 signatures. Chain integrity is verified automatically.

Cognigate controls maintenance personnel access through its role-based access model and cloud platform access controls: (1) Developer access — GitHub repository access is controlled via organization membership and team roles; branch protection prevents unauthorized changes to production code; (2) Operations access — Vercel team membership controls deployment and configuration access; Vercel's audit log tracks all team member actions; (3) Database access — Neon PostgreSQL access is controlled via database roles and connection credentials; database-level permissions enforce data access boundaries; (4) Administrative API access — the X-Admin-Key restricts maintenance operations on the running system to authorized administrators; (5) Personnel verification — all platform accounts (GitHub, Vercel, Neon) require individual identification and authentication. No physical facility access is required for Cognigate maintenance.
by-components.remarks: Inherited: Maintenance personnel access to physical infrastructure is controlled by Vercel/AWS. Cognigate controls software maintenance access through platform-level access controls and the X-Admin-Key authentication mechanism.

Cognigate's media protection policy addresses digital media (data at rest and in transit) as the system operates as a cloud SaaS with no physical media handling: (1) Data at rest — Neon PostgreSQL databases use encryption at rest (provided by Neon/AWS); proof chain records are integrity-protected via SHA-256 hashing and Ed25519 signatures; (2) Data in transit — all API communications use HTTPS/TLS 1.2+ enforced by Vercel's edge network; (3) Digital media lifecycle — proof chain records are retained for 7 years with immutable integrity; database backups are managed by Neon with configurable retention; (4) Policy documentation — media handling requirements are defined in the BASIS specification and operational procedures. Physical media protection is inherited from Vercel/AWS infrastructure controls.
by-components.remarks: Inherited: Physical media protection (disk encryption, media disposal, storage facility controls) is provided by Vercel/AWS. Cognigate implements digital media protection through encryption, integrity verification, and retention policies.

Cognigate restricts access to digital media through its authentication and authorization framework: (1) Database access — Neon PostgreSQL data is protected by database-level permissions and application-enforced access controls that restrict data access based on authenticated user identity and role; (2) Proof chain access — proof records are accessible only through the authenticated /v1/proof API endpoint; bulk export requires administrative authentication (X-Admin-Key); (3) Configuration data — environment variables and secrets are stored in Vercel's encrypted environment variable store, accessible only to authorized team members; (4) Source code — maintained in a private GitHub repository with organization-controlled access. Physical media access restrictions are inherited from Vercel/AWS data center controls.

Cognigate marks digital media (data classifications) through its capability taxonomy and trust tier system: (1) Data classification — the capability taxonomy defines data access tiers: sandbox (T0-T1), operational (T2-T3), classified (T4-T5), administrative (T6-T7), with each tier marking the sensitivity level of accessible data; (2) Proof chain marking — all proof records include metadata identifying the data classification level of the associated event (via the capability namespace: sandbox, data, comm, execute, financial, admin); (3) API response marking — API responses include trust tier context indicating the classification level of returned data. Physical media marking (labels, tags) is not applicable as Cognigate operates without physical media.
by-components.remarks: Inherited: Physical media marking is handled by Vercel/AWS for their storage infrastructure. Cognigate implements logical data classification through its capability taxonomy and trust tier system.

Cognigate's data storage is entirely digital, managed through cloud database services: (1) Primary storage — Neon PostgreSQL with encryption at rest, automated backups, and point-in-time recovery; (2) Proof chain storage — immutable records with SHA-256 hash chain integrity and Ed25519 signatures stored in the primary database with 7-year retention; (3) In-memory rate tracking — the velocity engine maintains request rate counters in application memory for real-time rate limiting. Physical media storage (locked cabinets, controlled areas) is inherited from Vercel/AWS data center controls.
by-components.remarks: Inherited: Physical storage protection (environmental controls, physical access) is provided by Vercel/AWS. Cognigate manages digital storage with encryption at rest, integrity verification, and configurable retention.

Physical media transport (courier, shipping) is not applicable as Cognigate operates without physical media.
by-components.remarks: Inherited: Physical media transport controls are not applicable. Cognigate protects digital data in transit through TLS encryption and cryptographic integrity verification of proof chain records.

Cognigate addresses media sanitization for its digital assets through cloud platform capabilities: (1) Database sanitization — Neon provides database deletion with storage-level sanitization handled by the underlying AWS infrastructure; (2) Entity deregistration — when an entity is deregistered, its authentication credentials are cryptographically invalidated (public key removed, API key hash deleted) while proof chain records are retained for 7-year compliance; (3) Log sanitization — Vercel manages log retention and sanitization per their data handling policies. Physical media sanitization (degaussing, destruction) is inherited from Vercel/AWS infrastructure.
by-components.remarks: Inherited: Physical media sanitization is performed by Vercel/AWS per their media sanitization procedures (aligned with NIST SP 800-88). Cognigate sanitizes digital assets through credential invalidation, cache expiration, and database-level operations.
